I did not like having the little sultana from the Leroux novel get replaced by the Shah's mother { or Sultan's mother I forget which since there were so many higher ups in that chapter }. Even not looking at it from a Leroux purist stand point it still didn't work for me. Sorry but it just didn't.

Next "chapter"

We're back to Erik again and transitioning from pre Leroux to where Leroux picked up in Erik's life. This is the point in the book where the awesomeness flattens out into okie dokieness and then starts its rapid downward spiral right near the end. I pretty well liked this chapter except for a few nitpicks here and there prolly until it came to the fateful scene where Erik sees Christine for the first time and my high opinion of the book crashes to the floor. As a prequel to the Leroux novel it's pretty stinking awesome but once she continues on to the point where Leroux picked up in his novel that's when I get disgusted. Although I have to admit Ayesha was a nice addition in this chapter. :)

Second half of the book where the awesomeness comes crashing down:

I had already heard this book wasn't completely Leroux accurate and had seen plenty of reviews from phans/fans who loved the first part of the book but hated the second part. It's safe to say that after having completely read this book I am now officially a part of that camp that prefers the first part and majorly dislikes the second part. This entire part of the review from here on out is one big humongous spoiler alert that I'm putting in here to prepare those who are major Leroux purists for the potential heart attack they may have after reading the second part of this book and also because I have some nit picks of my own that aren't specifically coming from a Leroux purist point of view.

First off I'm sorry but there was absolutely no real true chemistry between Erik and Christine. To me it felt more like they were majorly sexually attracted to each other and that was it { Susan Kay is very obviously Team Erik and Christine by this point in the book even though Christine does still end up with Raoul at the end of the book }. Honestly I liked Erik and Luciana's relationship waaaaay better then I liked the relationship between him and Christine and if the end of the "chapter" that involved Luciana had gone differently who knows but they might have ended up together and Christine would never have taken part in Erik's life the way she does in this book.
Also I still have no idea whether Erik completely sold his soul to the devil near the end of this part or not but he kept getting really close during this half of the book and that pissed me off that Kay thought he would do that.
Poor Don Juan Triumphant gets turned into the masturbatory song of the century *vomits all over the floor at this monstrosity of a destruction of that song* when Erik plays it of his own accord without any unmasking or anything in the next room while Christine gets it on with a pillow in her room { and yes Kay does get a bit descriptive as to what exactly is going on at that point -__- }.
The unmasking was crap. This is pretty much my summary of Kay's unmasking: Christine unmasks Erik. Erik goes "oh crap she unmasked me. Now what?" { yeah he's pretty freaking calm about it especially compared to the Leroux and even earlier unmaskings in the book from other people } then proceeds to have a seizure { because we all know he couldn't have been groveling at her feet for any other reason then that he was having a seizure } and then not long after that everythings all honky dory happy puppies and pretty unicorns land. Really? Really?! REALLY?!?!?!?!?!
Also no matter how much I wish Erik could have "gotten the girl" at the end of the book { or at least have lived long enough to move on and get another girl } Christine was NOT madly in love with Erik no matter what anyone says. I'm sorry but that's just how it is. { Just in case you were wondering Christine and I "don't get along well." }
Also Kay's pulls a Dance!Erik by having Christine look like Erik's mother. I'm not entirely crazy about that concept even in the Yeston & Kopit version of POTO and in this one it gets really really disgusting when I think how little Erik and Christine are attracted to each other personality wise in this version.
This is the part that makes my skin crawl the most when I think about how a later novel { think Phantom of Manhattan -__- } did this as well: Christine has Erik's kid { even better is how she gets it on with him pretty much the night before her wedding if I'm understanding things correctly which sounds incredible Love Never Dies now doesn't it? } and yes Christine does also die { quite a few years after Erik but still and she dies of cancer instead of a bullet a la Phantom of Manhattan and Love Never Dies but still Susan Kay tried to pull the whole "together in eternity" thing there when she had Christine die off from cancer years later.
I have to give credit where credits is due though that she did manage to not emasculate Raoul's character { whether it be figuratively a la Love Never Dies or figuratively and literally a la Phantom of Manhattan }.
Also three points to Susan Kay for making it no denying that Buquet hung himself in the torture chamber since there are those out there who say Erik killed him in the Leroux novel and I don't quite agree with that { though he really does in the Andrew Lloyd Webber version } so it was nice to see that in this novel.
I have other nitpicks as well but it's late and I don't have the time to list every last little nit pick.

P.S.
In the acknowledgements section at the end of the book Susan Kay mentions a 1967 cartoon version of Phantom of the Opera. That is a typo. It's actually 1987 just in case anyone wanted to go see it. It's easily found on youtube just by searching Phantom of the Opera cartoon.
top_rant.title: A Review Dividing The Book Into Two Halves

top_rant.text:
Hey Everyone, who's looking for an alternate ending?? Well check out Stephanie Ziel's alternate ending @ Addicted Readers: [...]

Its was AMAZING!!! I wish Veronica Roth would of written an ending like that!!

***UPDATED***

***REVIEW****

ALLEGIANT, Oh, ALLEGIANT, how you failed me in so many ways!

I went over every feeling, every thought, every emotion I had, be it good or bad. And after all that analyzing in my OWN opinion, I still came back with NOT being satisfied one bit with this Conclusion to my favorite series, or should I say "Used To Be My Favorite Series"?

FIRST LET ME TALK ABOUT MY FEELINGS ON THE ENDING:

WARNING A BUNCH OF SPOILERS:

***READ AT YOUR OWN RISK***
|
|
|
|
|
|
|
V
I had this book spoiled for me before I started reading ALLEGIANT, so I went into it already knowing what was going to happen to Tris in the ending. I found out about it 2 days before I started reading, and I was an emotional wreck! My husband thought I was insane! But I was stunned, I really could not believe Tris was going to die. I had no idea, not even a little thought that it would happen to Tris. So when I did find out, I was shocked! I was in denial for the first couple hours until finally I couldn't take it anymore and I broke down and read some of the reviews! Then, I was FORCED to see the truth, Tris would in fact die!! UGH, I still get teary eyed when I think about it! I never would of thought Veronica would do that to Tris, and not just to Tris, but to her LOYAL FANS! Tris had so many near death experiences, especially in INSURGENT, when she battled with death, and wanting to die, but finally she had an eye opener and realized she did not want to die, and that she was more then a self sacficer. So that led me to believe Tris had all the near death experiences that she was going to have, boy was I wrong!!!

I stand 100% behind MY Opinion, that this WAS THE WRONG WAY TO END THE DIVERGENT SERIES!!!

AGAIN, MY OPINION!!!

Veronica said that she had this ending planed from the very beginning of this series, and that it was the ONLY ending that she felt would do this series any justice! WHAT, NO, WRONG VERONICA! She said she felt having these characters end with a happy ending, holding hands, walking off into the sunset was fake and unbelievable. BUT wait, I came across an alternate ending to ALLEGIANT written by a Fan Fiction author Stephanie Ziel (POST HERE), and let me tell you, IT WAS AMAZING!!! And, NO, they did not have this sugarcoated happy ending like Veronica didn't want! They had a hard ending, that brought tears to my eyes, but still TRIS SURRIVED!!! And IN MY OPINION it was the BEST ENDING FOR THIS SERIES!!! If Veronica would of wrote this ending I think A LOT more people would of been more satisfied, including me!!

Now, all that said, I was still very upset that Tris died. I thought it was the wrong move for Veronica to make. (I am NOT the only one who thought this, as of the date of this review, Amazon's has a 2.8 Rating, Not good, not good at all). BUT I wasn't just upset that Tris died, I didn't like the WAY Tris died! I thought Tris sacrificing herself and taking her brothers place in the Weapons Lab, was the worst way for Tris to go! I mean I get it, this book is about selflessness and forgiving, and all that blah, blah, blah, BUT if Veronica Roth was going to kill off the main protagonist she should of at least had her go out with a bang, in which she DEFINITELY did not!

THESE ARE THE MANY OTHER REASONS I DID NOT LIKE ALLEGIANT:

Okay, the ending was not my only problem with ALLEGIANT. My first issue was the alternate POV's. I HATED IT!! It irritated me to no end! UGH, it drove me insane!! I could not tell Tobias and Tris apart to save my life. Only way I was able to tell them apart was when I went back to the beginning chapter and read the heading of who's POV it was. If not for the heading with the name of who's chapter it was, I would of been lost and waiting until whoever POV it was, said the other persons name. But their voice was so similar, that at times I just wanted to put the book down and scream, yes it was that bad for me!!

Another issue I had was Tobias! He was a totally different character from the first 2 books. I mean a different guy all together! ONE of the MANY reasons I LOVED Four was that he always had this sexy mysterious way about him, that just kept me wanting to know him more. And when I read he's POV in the Short Stories, I was in awe! I loved the tough, strong-willed, determined, guarded Four. And being in his mind was really such a treat! He had all these character traits that I loved so much in the Short Stories. So I was expecting to have the same in ALLEGIANT, but of course with more emotion because in the short stories he wasn't with Tris or in love. So I was curious to see how Four would react to being in love and having someone else to worry about besides himself. BUT WOW, what I got was A BIG FAT DISAPPOINTMENT!!!

At times Four acted naïve, like he was new too their world and all the plotting, scheming, betrayals, and death around him. It caused him many problems that he couldn't get out of! UGH, and he was WAY to trusting with the first pretty girl that feed him a load of crap! Since when did Tobias become so trusting with complete strangers that he was willing to go against everything he believed in? Not the Tobias I have grown to love and care deeply for!

Another thing that changed in ALLEGIANT with the character traits, was that Tris became the strong, sure of herself girl, and Four was the weak, trusting and unsure of who and where he belonged guy. And that was not the Four I've come to love! Nope, this new and NOT improved Four in ALLEGIANT --SPOILER-- was so trusting, that it even cost Uriah his life!! I'm not saying it was directly Four's fault, because it wasn't. But if he would of trusted Tris and her judgment, and not trusted Nita, and went along with her insane plan, then the whole mission would of had to wait, then Uriah wouldn't of been standing where he was when it all happened! So again, if Four would of went with his heart instead of his quick impulse, then certain things wouldn't of happened. Which honestly, I felt some thing's happened with really no reasoning behind it. Like Uriah, what was the whole purpose of killing him off??? Really I would love to know, because I can't figure it out. Again, he was another one of my favorite characters that got killed off, FOR WHAT???? Four was just so annoying, and most of the time I couldn't' wait to get back to Tris's POV. But even Tris had her annoying chapters too! I felt the total opposite about all of this in the first 2 books! I LOVED pretty much everything about DIVERGENT & INSURGENT! But ALLLEGIANT just fell flat for me in SO MANY WAYS, so more reasons then what I'm putting in this review!!! If I listed all the reasons why I hated ALLEGIANT, then we would be here all day, because their is SO many! Seriously!

Then the plot was so annoying, the whole what was outside of the fence was stupid, and definitely not what I was expecting! (I say that NOT in a good way) The GD's and GP's, was the worst idea Veronica has ever came up with! I was expecting something so unique and original from Veronica, because that's all she's ever given me! I kept waiting for the big BANG to happen when they went behind the boundaries of their city. I was hoping for a drawn out scary reason for them being horded off and locked away like dogs! But the whole Gene thing was just laughable! When I first found out the reasoning behind everything, I was just staring at the book, saying "What Did I Just Read? This Can Not Be It? Their HAS TO BE MORE"? I kept waiting for it all to be a big hoax's, but it wasn't, and I was let down for the hundredth time with this book! I just couldn't take it anymore. Then by the time I got to the ending, I just didn't have it in me to be mad, or upset, or cry anymore! I've had enough letdowns to last me a bookish lifetime just in this one book! And its SO HEARTBREAKING to feel this way about my favorite series!!!

I honestly can not think of one thing that I liked about this book! As of the beginning of this review I was planning on giving this book 2 stars, but now that I voiced some, NOT even ALL of my issues on paper, I realized that their really wasn't anything I enjoyed in this last book! So I feel obligated to give this book 1 star! And oh, how that makes me feel terrible! I really, really, really, wanted to love this book! I did, I really did, but I just can't for all the reasons stated above, and more not stated! I can't stress enough how much I LOVED the first 2 books, DIVERGENT & INSURGENT, and I had such high hopes for ALLEGIANT, maybe too high, because I was let down HARD!! I just can't love this book, or even like it, and I can't give it more then a 1 star rating! And I've never given a 1 star rating, NEVER! At least until this book! SO it is really hard for me to give it this 1 star, but it really does deserve it, in MY OPINION!

ON A FINAL NOTE:

So, I will walk away from this series heartbroken, torn,
lost, and unsure if I should ever put as much hope, faith and trust in another author the way I put forth in Veronica Roth! Will I read more of her books? I really don't know! She is such a skilled writer, that just took a big dive down in ALLEGIANT, but that does NOT make her any less of an author in my opinion. So I would hope Veronica would make ALLEGIANT a learning experience and learn from the many mistakes she made in this last book, and not make them again. So as of right now, who knows, depends on if I will ever feel inclined to pick up a book with Veronica Roth's name on the cover!

So there you have it, what I hated the most about ALLEGIANT! What are you thoughts on ALLEGIANT?? Did you love it, hate it, didn't really care either way?? But for all of you who feel that ending was the last straw with ALLEGIANT, then please take a moment and check out this alternate ending I have on my blog, as it has definitely made me feel so much better and more complete to walk away from this series with brighter thoughts!
top_rant.title: Who wants an ALTERNATE ENDING???? I DO, I DO!

top_rant.text:
I'm guessing Chichton would be pretty pissed that this was released with his name on it!

Warning: All kinds of details/spoilers if you're worried about that sort of thing because it's necessary to show the ridiculousness of this book so read either this review or the actual book at your peril!

Michael Crichton made a great career of producing incredible ideas with often mediocre characterization but although his characters were little more than stereotypes, the stories always had a logic to them. They made sense. This book does not make sense much of the time but I'm giving Crichton the benefit of the doubt and attributing this to Richard Preston. I have not read Preston's previous works of fiction and this book is not encouraging me to do so.
It starts with earnest grad student Peter being informed of the suspicious death of his brother Eric just prior to Peter and his fellow grad student scientists heading to Hawaii for job interviews with the company Eric worked for. He goes to Hawaii ahead of his classmates to investigate his brother's death(you know, as you do as a grad student) despite getting a text from his brother literally saying "Don't come!" While at the police station he is presented with video evidence that strongly implicates Alyson, vice president of his brother's employer Nanigen, so he naturally he decides to keep that and the preceding text to himself. To show that the author at least knows this doesn't make sense, he has the character repeatedly ask himself "Why did I do that?". His answer to himself?...."because we always looked out for each other as brothers." Wait...what? Not telling the police that you can identify someone who may have had something to do with your own brother's death is looking out for the dead sibling? I can only hope at this point to never become someone that Richard Preston wants to look out for!

But now we have to go with the premise that the genius scientist-grad student is also an idiot. He is then conveniently and magically(!) given recordings from the Alyson's cell phone and that confirm his worst fears again; she and her boss were involved in his brother's death! So now, armed with two forms of overwhelming recorded evidence and a text, he is more determined than ever to NOT involve law enforcement! Instead he devises a plan where he will trick the baddies into confessing during a guided tour so all the other visiting grad students will hear it and I guess, not having a brother to protect, go to the police in his stead.

The students embark on the guided tour designed to attract them to work for Nanigen during which Vin Drake, head Poo bah, explains that the Tensor Generator can shrink stuff. How cool! Still. not particularly wowed by this since they killed his brother and all, Peter subtly makes it known to the ne'er do wells that he is aware of the truth without actually saying anything specific in front of the tour group. You know, gotta protect his bro! Not pleased with this, Drake stops the tour and with everyone looking on, invites Peter to join him in another room where he proceeds to beat the crap out him. Not sure how Drake planned on explaining the wounds or the absence of the potentially never to be seen again Peter but it turns out since the author had other plans, the logic of that was never necessary to address. Problem solved!

Anyway, Peter succeeds beyond anyone's wildest dreams and the bad guy confesses over the loud speakers for the students and probably all kinds of employees hear as well! Woo Hoo! Book over in just a few pages! Oh, wait....there's more. Drake fakes an emergency and then runs into the room full of people that just heard him confess to killing his partner, Eric (and he's aware that they heard him!) in cold blood and says something along the lines of, "Come with me, I'll save you from this emergency involving dangerous robots!" So this room full of scientific geniuses unanimously decide to pick up their suddenly massively beaten colleague and follow the confessed killer and probable beater wherever he wants to take them without a word...instead of just going out the way they came. In fact other employees are running past them going the opposite direction literally yelling " Get outside!" but they follow Drake further into the building anyway. Free thinkers!

Drake leads them into a room clearly marked Tensor Generator(where have we heard that before?) and tricks them into standing on wacky hexagons on the floor by saying "Stand there, you'll be safe!" No one notices that the now exposed killer isn't trying to save himself by standing on a hex. He tells them those hexes are robot free zones or something and that is simply enough. He then leaves for the control room and locks them in. Then he opens the door again briefly and throws a Nanigen employee from the control room in with the students simply barking "Help them!" and then locks the door once more. He next appears in the window of the control booth obviously at the controls.

Now why this employee has no idea what to do during an emergency or what the alarms mean is curious to say the least. Why didn't he run screaming form the building like all those employees the group passed on the way to the Tensor Generator room? He seems to know that Drake means to shrink them all but doesn't seem to mind. That he would have no issue with being shrunken with the students during an emergency is weird since later it is revealed that as operator of that machine, he knew that prolonged shrunkeness would kill you and he had no way of knowing when or if the emergency would be resolved in a timely manner so this is also curious....or stupid. This book is populated by dim bulbs. Or as the incredibly hip Mr Drake said at one point, "Numskulls". Really? We're still saying that are we?

But the technician/corporate stooge/idiot rushes to a hex leading everyone to think that's where they need to be so they all position themselves. Then, in an unknown-emergency-inspired fit of kleptomania, grad student Karen King runs to retrieve a backpack from a wall full of them that she happens to notice hanging from a hook and then rushes back to her hex. No one has been told what is in the backpack or why it is there so it must have had a nice Hello Kitty design on it or something and she likey! And you can't say "Hey, she got the bag because she assumed it had post shrink tools or something!" because she didn't know she was about to be reduced to an insect. She thought she was standing there to be safe from robots. So why would she steal a backpack....in front of everybody?

Wammo! Tiny people ensue! Drake returns and decides to go all sadistic and kill them by feeding them to a snake. The idea being that there would be no evidence if they are consumed. I've never had a snake but it seems odd that a 4-5 foot snake that is used to eating rats would be interested in basically a half inch insect but the book says it is so who am I ask contradict it? 'Luckily', one of the grad students brought a compound to show off with in hopes of getting hired and slips it to Peter as he is thrown in with the reptile 'hoping' it will help fend off the snake. Now I could go all nit-picky and ask whether since they had been reduced to, I dunno, 1/1000000th of their original size to the point that they couldn't be heard and had nearly no mass, that the 'Hexanol' used to save Peter from the snake would probably have no effect since it was likewise reduced but I don't really know the science. It just seems logical. Still, I'm willing to concede the science fiction part of these types of stories as long as they don't break the rules they set for themselves in the context of the tale. So I plug onward!

Aaaaanyway, Unfortunately Drake is called away before he can actually see Peter get eaten by the reptile in a convoluted scene where an Ensign from the Coast Guard shows up to inquire about moving the boat Eric was seen jumping to his death from to a different boat yard for some reason. You see, they need the dead owner's approval and for that they go to his old job....like you do, Instead of the police or family. Maybe the Ensign is also Eric's brother and wants to protect him. I don't know if that is how it's done but it seems like a pretty lousy way to secure evidence to me. Anyway, while there as a device to get Drake out of the room, he lets loose with a torrent of information about what the cops are doing and where they are in their investigation. He even is able to say that the police weren't looking for a specific items on the boat! Apparently the HPD not only immediately notify the Coast Guard of all their progress during an investigation, they even notify all of the lowly Ensigns(No disrespect. Thank you for your service!)! Heck, he even knows that the cops will be at that same facility ANY SECOND to search his office! Who needs a scanner when you have the Coast Guard? The Ensign is invited by Drake to stay until the police arrive and even come along when they search Eric's office. Not sure if the cops want an extra CG member in the room when they are investigating but the Ensign thinks it's a good idea so he stays.

Here's how the conversation morphs from the Ensign rightfully not knowing anything to having every detail:

Drake-"I thought the police were still inspecting the boat"
Ensign - "I wouldn't know about that" The police hadn't come to see him, it was the boat yard owner. - doesn't know anything.
Drake - " I heard they were looking for a phone"
Ensign - "Not that I'm aware, I think the police have finished their investigation" - slowly changing from knowing nothing to having inside info.
Ensign - "At least as soon as they complete their inspection of his office" - Now he knows the inner workings of the investigation!
Ensign - " I know they went to (Eric)Jansen's apartment today and they're coming to look at his office here....any minute now." Suddenly he knows everything including time tables for an organization he is not affiliated with to the extent that they pull up seconds later as if his watch had been synchronized to the Honolulu PD directly!!
Another detour into something I can't speak of with confidence but sounds weird is that CFO Alyson is in her office watching the whole thing through the security cameras that are fed to her desk monitor. This seems odd to me but maybe ultra high tech offices now offer security camera access to all of their top employees. I digress.

The conflicted Ms Alyson, who apparently is a proponent of the 'we should all get to murder one person' rule, because she was fine killing Peter's brother HERSELF via remote control now but suddenly has an issue with multiple homicides so she rescues the helpless minis. This will greatly anger Vin the Merciless whom she knows is determined to kill even her if she betrays him but she proceeds to try to save them anyway because as it says in the book "She had to stop him from killing those kids!"...by driving them to the ocean..... to drown them. Wait...what?
Okay to be fair she decides to save them and then while driving her corporate BMW, she decides to kill them again and then when confronted by Drake, she can't do it, so intends to set them free in the nice, safe Hawaiian rain forest full of who knows what kinds of bugs and creatures that eat little things that are half an inch tall.

Meanwhile Drake decides to kill her but then while driving his corporate midnight blue Bentley convertible decides he "couldn't kill her, he couldn't afford to kill her, he needed her on his side" because it'd be too many deaths or disappearances at one time and would provoke a thorough investigation which would likely discover "things that he had done" and so once it's clear that he can't kill her....he kills her.

But before that, while still at the lab, Drake concludes that Alyson must have the shrunken students in her purse so he scrambles to follow her as she leaves the building. He follows her and eventually confronts her in his 'not going to kill her' mode. He deviously manipulates her into letting them go in the woods but they escape by cutting through the bag they are in and fall ....into the woods. Woo! That was close! They were almost left in the woods against their will! Fortunately, they escaped into the woods of their own accord! She panics and despite the fact she was going to leave them there anyway is worried that they will be hurt or even KILLED! Oh, the humanity!

Drake tells her that the only way out now is to make it look like the grads were out drinking and drove off a really high cliff by planting the MANY personal items belonging to the grad students in it that he *surprize* collected at some point while trying to rush out the door and follow her from the corporate building. He had to rush you see, because it was night time and he was going to have to follow her lights so when exactly he had the time to formulate this plan and gather the items is unclear. He is one fast dude! Still not sure exactly why Drake would give a bunch of prospective employees a BENTLEY to drive around in either. I know I've never been given the corporate car while waiting for the results of a job interview but I hear Hawaii is laid back and that's the story he's going with so.......

He then gets all OCD on her about placing the items where each SPECIFIC student would be sitting in the car because he insists they have to 'stage' the crime scene. How he knows which objects belong to which student having only just met them for a few moments before going postal isn't exactly explained. Maybe in the few seconds he had gather all the items he also took time to watch surveillance video so he could jot down what belonged to whom. He is fast, you know. How these items would stay in place as the car plummets of a 600 foot cliff and into the water and how this is supposed to be important to the police when they potentially find the car is also not exactly clear either. Why it wouldn't be weird that the items be there when ALL six bodies are gone seems a little strange and Alyson mentions this but it is a convertible and with the top down anything could happen I guess and whatever, he's an evil guy and they do wacky things, right? RIGHT??!?!? Alyson is not without her wackiness here too. While being scared to death the police will see through this lame ruse, she can't help but be concerned that one of the items belonging to a student used in staging, a belt made from python, is illegal! She must be one heck of a CFO! She is comforted by Drake who assures her it is only illegal in California. Whew! Let's get back to covering up our probable mass murder!

At this point it seems the idea is that with no top to the car, the missing bodies will not be an issue but what do I know? Nothing as it turns out. He has her drive his Bentley convertible to the edge of the cliff and then puts on gloves, I'm assuming because the fact that it's his car and he was just driving it a minute ago, the gloves would eradicate all his fingerprints from every time he's ever driven the car? Or he was afraid of leaving finger prints on Alyson's skull as he kills her, apparently no longer in 'I need her alive and on my side' mode. Because Drake asks her to get back in the car to drive it literally ten feet so they can then push it off the cliff. She says she doesn't want to and would like him to do it but he demurs saying that he would have to move the seat and THEN the police would be suspicious!!!! She accepts this logic easily because everyone knows, once you move a seat, it's set for life!!!! She then gets back in and drives the long ten feet toward the cliff. He then has her PUT UP THE TOP of the convertible, the only thing that would've reasonably explained why none of the bodies were there, to keep the student's items inside! At this point I am rooting for her slow agonizing death myself. Put her out of my misery; she is too stupid to live!

Meanwhile back in the rain forest, the little grad students get the history of the shrinking project from the Nanigen employee and then take stock of what they have to survive. Karen says "I took a backpack before he shrunk us!" raising more questions that no one asks. Again, why did she take the pack if she didn't know she was about to be shrunk? If the Nanigen employee DID know they were going to be shrunk since he directed everyone to get on the hexes, why DIDN'T he take one? Or tell them all to? If Karen DID know she was going to be reduced, why did she return to the hex at all instead of telling everyone else to get off of the ones they were on to save them? Did she really believe Drake was shrinking them to protect them even after so callously admitting to killing Eric and apparently beating Peter to a pulp just prior? Is she stupid or a thief or stupid? Is she related to Alyson? Is the Nanigen employee stupid or just stupid? Tough call!

After this the group embarks on what is basically a paint by numbers sci-fi escapade with crazy monsters and of course the mandatory extremely annoying member of the group that fights with everyone and then turns on them exposing them to danger because he believes that Drake, who has been trying to kill them the entire book, will suddenly want to help him because he's injured. 'I was going to kill you but now that I see you are hurt, let's get you to a doctor!' is what he's presuming. No real need to go into anymore detail than that. It was basically the first third of the book that was so obscenely ridiculous!

Why did I read this thing? I couldn't stop! It was so bad to begin with that I had to see if it ever made sense! Is that so wrong?

But in case you were wondering, it's set up perfectly for a sequel! I can't wait!
top_rant.title: This book is so bad, I had to finish it!

top_rant.text:
John Romita Sr. takes over art duties, and the series continues to modernize. This is the era I love most. Romita’s best art is in the first half of this omnibus, but it is great all the way though. The series blends fun and hardships far better in this era. This series features the debuts of the Rhino, the Shocker, and the Kingpin! It also has some amazing returns from Doctor Octopus, the Vulture, and Mysterio!

Amazing Spider-Man #39 ⧫ 4 Stars “How Green Was My Goblin!”
I forgot how quickly Harry and Gwen relax once Romita shows up. The college issues are really more defined from here than earlier. Also, the Green Goblin returns, and he is shockingly revealed! At least, he was. It’s still a little silly, but this is a classic.

Amazing Spider-Man #40 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “Spidey Saves the Day!”
This is a climactic fight that has its own level of silliness, though what really bogs it down is there is way too much exposition and dialogue. Stan needed to calm down. Also, this begins the Goblin’s amnesia that will influence his status quo for this era. Also, it’s not the last amnesia…

Amazing Spider-Man #41 ⧫ 4 Stars “The Horns of the Rhino!”
The first original villain of this era is the Rhino! He’s pretty fun, and I think his build up and continuing presence over the next few issues are what distinguish him as a threat and make him memorable. Pete and Betty meet again and officially end their relationship on a more positive note. Also, John Jameson returns to become more of a recurring supporting character. This continues the more lax social bleedover that makes this era of Spider-Man feel much more modern.

Amazing Spider-Man #42 ⧫ 4 Stars “The Birth of a Super-Hero!”
So, this issue’s villain is the worst part of it. Everything else surrounding that is far more significant, including the college crew continuing to form. Oh, and possibly the most iconic Spider-Man panel ever: “Face it, tiger… You just hit the jackpot!” Yep, the actual intro of the amazing Mary Jane!

Amazing Spider-Man #43 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “Rhino on the Rampage!”
Mary Jane immediately sets herself apart, and this issue even works with a retcon that comes later. This wraps up the Rhino and preps the return of the Lizard. Why so highly rated? I think Romita’s style is really beginning to click, though his paneling will get better. It’s just a good handoff that feels like what this era is remembered for.

Amazing Spider-Man Annual #3 ⧫ 2 Stars “...To Become An Avenger!”
Ugh, the Avengers are jerks in this, and it’s just not a very fun issue to read with so much procedure and legalism. Some have pointed out that the moment of understanding Spider-Man has with the Hulk is great, but that’s one page. I wouldn’t join the Avengers either.

Amazing Spider-Man #44 ⧫ 4 Stars “Where Crawls the Lizard!”
The Lizard returns as Mary Jane joins the college crowd! It’s a little more like the Ditko era in the ironic downer ending of this and the next Lizard story. The continued formation of the supporting cast, both the college crowd and Conners’ family, continue to fuel my enjoyment of this era.

Amazing Spider-Man #45 ⧫ 4 Stars “Spidey Smashes Out!”
Spider-Man defeats the Lizard in an original way and just more good fun from the supporting cast! From here on, the action panels are large and intricate. I love the way Romita distinguishes distinct action from the close up dialogue scenes. It’s something that he continues for the rest of the run.

Amazing Spider-Man #46 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “The Sinister Shocker!”
The second new villain of the era, the Shocker has become something of a joke in recent years, but he’s rather cool here. I like how well thought out the suit is and the fact that the combat is very well thought out. Also, there’s been a Bugle reported named Foswell who’s become suspicious of Peter who is also dealt with cleverly in this issue.

Amazing Spider-Man #47 ⧫ 4 Stars “In the Hands of the Hunter!”
This is an iconic issue because of the hangout scene, a defining feature of the era, and Kraven’s attack on the hangout. Also, Kraven has laser nipples… Just touching on the important stuff.

Amazing Spider-Man #48 ⧫ 4 Stars “The Wings of the Vulture!”
I might just have to give it up and say the Vulture is one of my favorite villains. even here with the silly new vulture, I rather like him. The snow really helps distinguish this issue, though it begins the disturbing trend of villains not checking if Spider-Man is dead and just assuming it. It’s annoying, but I forgive it because I love everything else.

Amazing Spider-Man #49 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “From the Depths of Defeat!”
Believe it or not, this is the first time Spidey’s faced two major supervillains at the same time, unless you count the Goblin and Crime-Master back in issues 26-27. Thus, it’s more fighting, which aren’t my favorite issues, but this one is unique enough.

Amazing Spider-Man #50 ⧫ 5 Stars “Spider-Man No More!”
This issue is iconic, and I can’t really disagree. Both the cover and the page #8 splash have been reproduced countless times. It does feel a little out of nowhere because it’s touching on the endings from issues #43-45 and #47, not the last few issues since Spider-Man has been too hurt/injured. So, it just feels a little weird. Otherwise, it’s awesome, and it ends on the introduction of the Kingpin! In other words, it holds up as the best issue since #33.

Amazing Spider-Man #51 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “In the Clutches of the Kingpin!”
This issue establishes Kingpin as a real threat. It’s back to the blend of personal and superheroes, and I think this continues in the fine tradition of early Spidey three-parters.

Amazing Spider-Man #52 ⧫ 4 Stars “To Die a Hero!”
So, the biggest issue here is Kingpin not removing Spider-Man’s mask and using a death trap. It’s some sillier 60s stuff that is a little out of place. I suppose one could say the Kingpin is arrogant and wants their drowning deaths to look like an accident, but it is the weakest part of the trilogy. It does have some good J.J.J. and Foswell stuff.

Amazing Spider-Man Annual #4 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “The Web and the Flame!”
Okay, this is another use of the movie making trap trope, but it also displays what I think is the first use of Spider-Man villains having backup plans. Also, I love the Human Torch.

Amazing Spider-Man #53 ⧫ 4 Stars “Enter: Dr. Octopus”
Some good, classic Spider-Man here with Spidey’s personal life leading into his superhero life. Also, there’s some Professor Warren and Gwen interaction that read very differently based on some later events.

Amazing Spider-Man #54 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “The Tentacles and the Trap!”
The series continues and gets way more fun. Some people like to make fun of this issue, but I actually love how Doc Ock showing up at Peter’s home goes. It feels like a more committed version of the Goblin’s visit in #39.

Amazing Spider-Man #55 ⧫ 4 Stars “Dock Ock Wins!”
I feel like I say a lot of similar things for this era: great art, good blend of plots, and cool villains. This one has all that with a little complication at the end that I’ll talk about next time.

Amazing Spider-Man #56 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “Disaster!”
I gave some crap for the Goblin amnesia, but I like the Spider-Man loss of identity two-parter. It allows a look at what a lonely existence Spider-Man would have without the others as well as provides some drama.

Amazing Spider-Man #57 ⧫ 4 Stars “The Coming of Ka-zar!”
The J. Jonah stuff is a little repetitive, but otherwise, I like the conclusion to the amnesia arc. Ka-Zar is a logical guest start and doesn’t feel too intrusive.

Amazing Spider-Man #58 ⧫ 2.5 Stars “To Kill A Spider-Man!”
Ugh, I hate Spider-Slayers, and this is no different. The one thing that’s changed is that Smythe is crazy now. The continuing supporting cast stuff is the main reason to read this.

Amazing Spider-Man #59 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “The Brand of the Brainwasher!”
This story gets some crap because it is a little silly, but I rather like it. The epilogue to the amnesia storyline starts the issue, and I appreciate the character development for Peter, Captain Stacy, and Gwen. For a silly story, it has a killer cliffhanger.

Amazing Spider-Man #60 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “O, Bitter Victory!”
This is also the closest that the series has gotten to refuting the Mary Jane retcon, and Mary Jane’s movements in this storyline are actually rather confusing. She just disappears after talking to Captain Stacy and shows up next issue like nothing happened. Peter and Gwen fall out here for relatively good reasons that turn a little sour next issue.

Amazing Spider-Man #61 ⧫ 3 Stars “What a Tangled Web We Weave...!”
The series ends weaker than I’d like with more deathtraps from Kingpin and Peter being an idiot again. Just explain to Gwen!

Amazing Spider-Man #62 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “Make Way for... Medusa!”
Romita wanted to draw Medusa, so he concocted a fun little plot to do it. It’s fun

Spectacular Spider-Man Magazine #1
“Lo, This Monster” ⧫ 2.5 Stars
This story is very out of place in the Omnibus as it completely ignores the state of Gwen and Peter’s relationship. However, I can’t seem to find anywhere that it could really go… Honestly, this pseudo-Frankenstein political drama is only worth reading for the art.

Amazing Spider-Man #63 ⧫ 4 Stars “Wings in the Night!”
Vulture vs. Vulture with Spidey in the middle. I’m actually really glad the original Vulture returned. Though his escape is… shaky. It creates a cool story that escapes the diminish returns that villains can have.

Amazing Spider-Man #64 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “The Vulture's Prey”
Ugh, I’m tempted to rate this down for MJ’s terrible haircut. The rest of this is great, though. The Bugle setting and the cliffhanger ending are classic.

Amazing Spider-Man #65 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “The Impossible Escape!”
Spider-Man and Captain Stacy escape prison. It’s another creative setup with fantastic use of setting and tone.

Amazing Spider-Man #66 ⧫ 4 Stars “The Madness Of Mysterio!”
I’ve given some crap to Mysterio’s previous plans, but I like this one. Also, another great cliffhanger.

Amazing Spider-Man #67 ⧫ 4.5 Stars “To Squash A Spider!”
Cool setup, cool conclusion. True, Mysterio’s skill is still questionable, but it looks great!

Spectacular Spider-Man Magazine #2 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “The Goblin lives!”
Infinitely better than the first and a somewhat unique Goblin story, though it doesn’t stand out as much as many others. This is the last of the magazine format, and I think it’s for the best as it seems to mostly need to have disposable stories.

Amazing Spider-Man Annual #5 ⧫ 3.5 Stars “The Parents of Peter Parker!”
Peter Parker’s parents become kind of a mess, but this is a neat little story. I am suspicious about how much Peter’s solution will work, but I guess I’m not a spy.
top_rant.title: Spider-Man at his Most Fun!

top_rant.text:
***Review posted on The Eater of Books! blog***

The Debutante is Mine by Vivienne Lorret
Book One of the Season's Original series
Publisher: Avon Impulse
Publication Date: April 12, 2016
Rating: 4 stars
Source: Copy sent by the publisher

***Warning: this is an adult book, and for the eyes of mature readers***

Summary (from Goodreads):

From the Season Standard: “A true Season’s Original embodies the class, grace, and style of the ton. Such an honor ensures the recipient her pick of eligible suitors…”

Lilah Appleton’s prospects are looking dim. With one last chance to find a titled husband before she’s forced to wed her wretched cousin, she must make this Season count. Plain, forgettable Lilah must become the Season’s Original. Desperate, she seeks help from the devilishly charming, untitled, and thoroughly unsuitable Jack Marlowe. All she must do now is resist the tempting rogue…

Bastard son and self-made man, Jack Marlowe loathes the aristocracy. When he meets Lilah, he expects her to be like all the other greedy husband-hunters. But she’s far more dangerous. Her alluring smiles and sharp tongue intrigue him. Before he knows it, he agrees to help her find a husband, revealing tricks to ensnare any man. The only problem is, his plan works too well—on him.

When Lilah becomes the belle of the ball, Jack realizes he may lose her forever—unless he can take a chance on love and claim his debutante...

What I Liked:

I received a copy of this book via the Avon Addicts program, sponsored by the publisher! In no way did this affect my opinion of the book.

I've not read a book by Lorret! I've seen her historical romance novels around, and some very positive reviews for her books. I knew that eventually, being the HR lover that I am, I would read one of her books. This one was an excellent start! I adored this sweet, enjoyable novel.

At twenty-three, Lilah Appleton is at her third and most likely final Season. At the end of this Season, if she doesn't land a rich, titled husband, she'll be forced to marry her cousin, in order to keep the family estate/lands/money within the family... as decreed by her controlling, verbally abusive father (who is dead before the beginning of this book). When Lilah meets Jack Marlowe, she knows better than to engage with the likes of him. His reputation is quite known - he likes women, and he makes a lot of money. He's the bastard son of an already rich duke, but he wants nothing to do with his father. What better way to learn how to charm noblemen than to enlist the help of a notorious rake who is estranged from the ton?

Ahhhh! Did I mention that this book was cute? It was! Sweet, cute, adorable, with touches of angst and "real" issues that gave the story and characters depth, but weren't overwhelming. I LOVE that this book is over one hundred pages shorter than your average HR novel. If you're looking for a sweet and steamy HR novel that won't take forever to read, here is one!

On the note of this novel being short - the length of the story did not hinder the development of the story or the characters or the conflict! Lorret does a marvelous job of telling the story and keeping the pacing steady and yet engaging. We know that time passes in this book, even if the book does not have scenes for each day in the hero and heroine's life since meeting. Days and weeks pass in this book, so no need to worry about rushed development!

Lilah is such an adorable protagonist! She is so determined to find a titled gentleman this Season - and with good reason. She has her hopes set on Lord Ellery, who lives close to where her family's estate is (Lilah is currently living with an aunt). But sadly, literally everyone (man or woman) seems to forget Lilah exists, or that they were introduced to Lilah in the past, or that Lilah is in the room too. She thinks of herself as plan and unforgettable. But she has a lot of self-esteem and confidence, and she has a fiery tongue when she isn't stuffing herself behind society rules. I adore her!

But Jack - Jack is easily my favorite character of the book. Oh Jack! He's the only son of a very important and rich duke (who's name and title I've forgotten), and he's the duke's bastard son. The duke married and had eight daughters, all much younger than Jack, and none of which can inherit. For the first ten years of his life, Jack had a very difficult life. His father wouldn't marry his mistress (Jack's mother), so the mistress had to raise her son on her own, until Jack was ten, and she sent him to his father, so that the duke would send Jack to school. And off to Eton Jack went. Jack has held bitterness toward his father for years, even though the duke and duchess want nothing more than for Jack to "come home" and accept his place with them, as heir, and son.

I don't blame Jack! His childhood was rough, and when he left for Eton, he felt terrible for leaving his mother (and he was only ten!). Jack is a charming, wickedly charming young man, and Lilah sees him as a warrior. He is a large guy, and he's a self-made man. He's a curiosity in polite society (as a bastard son), and so he avoids society. Until Lilah, who intrigues him. Suddenly, you can find Jack at all the social events! Which makes for HILARIOUS scenes, trust me.

I love how lighthearted this story is, so funny and cute! Lilah and Jack have a hate-to-love relationship (well, at least on Lilah's side). I liked seeing them dance around each other (so to speak)!

I mentioned Jack's issues with his father - honestly, I loved this "angst" in the story. It's really not that angsty, but I loved it. There were two scenes with Jack and his father that BROKE my heart in pieces - poor Jack! Poor duke! I love how Lorret wrote those scenes. I wanted to give everyone a hug!

You see why I loved Jack, right? He's such a charming alpha, but he has daddy issues. I don't know why that package is so appealing to me, but it is!

The romance is sweet! There is chemistry between Jack and Lilah. Unfortunately there is only one sex scene (booo), but given the length of this novel (less than three hundred pages), that is to be expected, I suppose. Still, there were some subtly steamy scenes in this book! Not many though.

Overall, I really liked this novel. I picked it up on a whim and I regret nothing!

What I Did Not Like:

Obviously I'm going to mention the lack of sex scenes! Of course I am. It's weird because I liked how this book was shorter than your average HR novel, but I definitely wanted more sexy times!

A note that is more to the publisher than author - the print of this paperback was SO small. Like, even smaller than the text size of your average HR novel. I have great vision but I was totally squinting!

Would I Recommend It:

I highly recommend this novel, if you're in the mood for HR but don't have the time for one of those four hundred page bad boys! Length of book almost never bothers me; however, I appreciated the fact that this book was short! It was adorable. And the ebook is very cheap!

Rating:

4 stars. My first venture into Lorret books was a success! I definitely want to continue the series. I believe Wolford (who's name is LIAM!) is next, and then Max/Juliet! I am super SUPER excited for Max/Juliet's book. So many secrets between those two. Need!
top_rant.title: Review for The Debutante is Mine by Vivienne Lorret

top_rant.text: The writing of this book Fatal Vision took so much research I'm sure . But the writing seemed to write with such ease its as if he were right their taking notes for all the contents in the book !! Including the murders . As a little girl I saw the movie and was aware of what happened in the Sharon Tate murders as well . Even as a little girl I thought about Sharon and her murder , with her being pregnant , as something that shocked me ! Jeffery Macdonalds murders the same thing . I was aware of this book as a little girl - the murders even though they hapoened before I was born - but they continued to be talked about afterwards for a decade and of course on and on - thats how I was ably to see the movie . My sister and I were very aware type kids . She was born in 1971 but still these murders as Sharon Tate and the murders in her house never stopped being talked about !! The man that plays Jeffrey Macdonald does too great a job :( !! He plays this person too good actually and as a little girl I hated him ! Yes because I didn't believe he did it  just because the scenario fit - I believed then with my whole heart with how the story was presented that he did kill his family ! And I still do !! Especially after reading THIS book !! This book is so objectively written but so intricate the person writing it was just possessed to piece it as he went along PERFECTLY !!! He gave me the same view of this Jeffery Macdonald as I saw in the movie only worse and more intricate !! It's not because Collette is a woman and I'm a woman that I believe he did it either . But this book lets you see and hear play for play what was really important to Jeffery Macdonald . It was not his wife and kids that's for sure . And the way he LIES !! I mean he seriously should have thought that lie out more . But he chose a night and new he had to kill his family whether an argument ensued or not ! He knew it was now or never . He never wanted kids or that kind of lifestyle . He wanted a flashy life . Although he was capable of telling you how both his children's personalities were - Kimberly being " soft and feminine " kristy being two years old and yet she was a little " toughy " sticking up for her " big " sister when neighborhood kids would tease her . Still my father can recite things about my sister and I the same way it does not mean that their endearment their . It doesn't mean they wouldn't kill you or pay child support . In this case he said all these endearing things that he remembered but it was not enough to stop the murder and my father didnt pay child support yet he was an excellent father when I was very little then he disappeared . So that's a bunch of crap ! It baffles me that now that we have the Internet their are people that feel they can do a better job at deciding that this man didnt kill his family !! His personality and how he views people especially women in his life that he was intimate with show a man with a set mind on certain things that pertain to his life . Dependent is one . That word sort of turned him on it seems ! He did these horrific murders . Wanted Collete's family support and thank GOD they caught on !!!! But he really did not want their support he was just scared what they would do if it came out in their lifetime while they had any physical strength left themselves !! Why would he think they would stop grieving their daughter and grandchildren ?? When people forgive in an instance where their children have been murdered they don't always forgive the way some idiots do by baking cookies for the mirderer ! Sharon Tate's family all died from cancer because of how this ate away at their very core souls ! They NEVER got over her death !!! And the reason is because a lot of times the personality of the person killed was too good for this world but still WE are not GOD to make the decision to take them off this universe ! GOD even knows we need good examples down here on this universe !!! The way Collettes personality was described was that of a good kind person . She could have been stubborn in a sweet way too . But for Jeffrey Macdonald to underestimate his family is beyond me . Or any story similar to this one . But this one especially . He could have abruptly left Collette and said I don't want to be married or have kids anymore . That is that simple . He underestimated that they would not have been able to move on ! His children would have moved on . In speaking of Collette coming from a family where her own father commited suicide I still don't believe she would have done the same thing . At least I hope she would not . His oldest daughter Kimberly who he said was so soft and feminine would have rose to the occasion many times in her life had she been allowed the chance to ! I mean she was an Aries !! He over kilked kristy , the baby  because she was so tough even as a two year old that she put her little hands up to fight the killer blows of her assailant - her father . I'm sure she made the most noise ! He lied so bad in telling his story that I would have just broke down an admitted to the murders ! He said their were four people in front of him ??? But could hear his wife saying " oh Jeff why are they doing this to me !! What she was saying is why are you doing this to me ! She watched with her HEART the man she LOVED the father of her children kill her and her babies !!! It's like the first time the knife struck near Sharon Tate's stomache how she must have felt when she knew her killers didnt care a out her or her baby she screamed " oh noooooo ' because the baby she was two weeks before having was about to die ! Same for Collette ! In her instance not just the baby in her stomache but she saw with her eyes that he was capable of killing their children and she was a witness to that - that this mean meant business - his business . His life was more important . To underestimate his family ??? To underestimate what they meant to her family !!! Her step father !!! Her mother who was heartbroken to the very end !! Not every person who has to forgive bakes cookies for the murderers of their families !!!!! Not everyone that has a loved one murdered marries the murderer !!! They are infected with these murders for life !!! Infected !!! Collette was a Taurus she would have found another beautiful man and she would have got tough . She would have moved on and especially under the careful watch of her loving family!!! Kimberly would have moved on and she would have been a toughy herself . She may have the DNA of her moms traits genetics of her soft feminine side but she would have come into her own and she would have defended he little sister herself !!! Kristy would have been able to defend her mom and her sister in the future if he came around harassing them about clearing up his image for walking out ! Had he at least just let them live and just say I don't want to live this life I want to live a flashy life . To underestimate Collette , Kimberly , Kristin ??? It's beyond me - they would have MOVED ON !!! He thought he was too wonderful for them to move on . Now we have people who actually believe that maybe he didnt kill his family ??? Now that we are able to do what I'm doing now , posting how I feel ???  He killed them !! All three ! When u look at the crime scene photos ??? Oh my gosh !!! Her little hand is lifted up a bit as if she tried to get up ????  I cried watching the movie mostly because he could do this to them under no circumstance ? He just knew he had to do it and the first blow sealed the deal !! This isn't just about the physical murders . No it's about Jeffery Macdonalds mind . How he talks and what he talks about and how he explains situations about  his life, his persona is very transparent . It's seems Mr. McGinnis the writer leaves  all the evidence of his persona and we take it from there . Depending on the sickness of our own minds we can tell what is truth here . My sister read this book when she was 14 and said it was great ! I read it now as an adult and she said you are going to like that book because its a page turner . We also as I said saw the movie . We sneaked and saw it on TV . When someone asked me what part are you reading of the book now I say I'm reading the part where this doctor husband is lying . I despise his lie the most . How he lies and how even the most unaffected person can see he lied . I never give the best reviews . I prefer to debate in person because I always win . I also never follow . But I have too too much to say when it comes to this story because I sympathize with Collette , her children , her mother , her step father , her brother . Collete was 26 years young ? Her children were still babies . They would have moved on from this man . I know it .
top_rant.title: This book was an A ➕➕➕!!

top_rant.text:
When I was in High School my tweedy, Shakespearean teacher wrote on my Permanent Record Card that, "( I ) was obsessed with WEIRD, FANTASTIC STORIES" - not much of a favorable evaluation for a young fellow preparing for a college career in the Sciences. I learned of this comment many years later when my college advisor did me the favor of leaving his office door open one Saturday morning so that I could conveniently look up my records in his file cabinet. In High School and later into adulthood I followed up on his pronouncement and dutifully read through Burroughs, Haggard, Lovecraft, Hodgson, Hogan, Poe (Comic Books - Oooooo! Not Them!!) etc. while my science education protected me from any harm. Although I did not particularly approve of Mr. E________'s evaluation in my permanent record I followed through on his ominous assessment. Unfortunately, I did not come across Kenneth Robeson and Doc Savage until much later in life. This goes to prove that Mr. E__________ was in part wrong - A true Fantasy Fanatic would have come across Doc much earlier.

That said, with my much more modest familiarity with the fantastic I seemed to have saved some of the very best fantasy until last (I am near eighty now and have only known of Doc for the last 20 years). Of late, I came upon a small collection the 1970's Bantam paperbacks (six) by way of a young fellow known to me only as "DOMINIC" who did the favor of writing his name inside the cover of of one of the six Bantams much later deposited in Abie Baby's Used Bookstore (by his mother?) for me to find. I had previously read some of the fancy reprints (cardboard covers, illustrations and newsy bits) but Dominic's somewhat wrinkled copies with tanned pages and old pulp smell proved perfect for drawing me in - or, as Oscar Wilde once wrote, "The pathways to the soul are the senses....." Dominic apparently only had these six (and one other not included) - since he had only checked off these titles on the listing provided on the back cover and on one of the interior pages of his copy of "The Magic Island" (originally published as "Ost" - Who would want to ignore that?). Now I am reading "The Magic Island" and it is a perfect "TREASURE!!"

Of course, perhaps your High School mentor did not say you were "obsessed with the fantastic" but to appreciate my ardor for the little (suitably cheap) wrinkled (quite appropriately) copy that Dominic bequeathed to me it would not hurt you at all. In fact, it would definitely do wonders!!! I do not think that Mr. E___________ would approve "The Magic Island" as "literature" along with his moldering volumes I would peruse in his classroom cabinet during after school hours. Henry James proclaims that literature must contain characters who are "vessels of experience" and must be able to articulate the meaning of such experience for the betterment of the greater part of humanity. Doc Savage and his pals are as Monty Python's J. Cleese would put it, "... something. completely different." MONK, HAM, RENNY et. al. are idiosyncratic fellows both in personality and appearance with either strength or intelligence which comes into play in aiding Doc in solving crimes based on a few forensic hints. Their names are allegorical and their behaviors get them involved in fascinating locales and situations regardless of their family histories and "experiences" beyond those which allow them to do their work effectively as kinds of "scientists." Flaubert and Dostoevsky (among others) develop the concept that words and syntax can only create "fiction" since the "consciousness" exists outside of material reality (or only in the realm of "possibility" within the mathematical information of which the Universe is most fundamentally composed. ) Logic and philosophy exist only within the consciousness and these ideas exert no restraints on this reality. Einstein puts the matter succinctly, "The best ideas are created wholly within the human mind without reference to observable objects. " Thus, words or letters are used as symbols for unseen particulars - mass, energy, momentum and enthalpy, etc., etc. manipulated by the rules of mathematics. Poetry only among all literary techniques approaches mathematics in the manipulation of unseen particulars. The objects of Science are two fold: first the accounting for facts, and second the formulation of reliable predictions. Therefore, the terse writing of Lester Dent as the author of the Savage Sagas approaches mathematics in its simplicity and limits itself to unseen particulars manipulated by the story characters in accounting for events (facts) and making reliable predictions (what happens) as in mathematical calculations. Fiction provides a wonderful way to "rot your mind" as Harvey Kurtzman used to say in his wonderful, old ten cent MAD MAGAZINES of the day as he parodied the silly, human views of reality. Even Mr. E________ did admit (grudgingly) when I spoke on MAD in classroom "reports" that MAD did seem to "appeal to the more intelligent students!!!!" So much for "obsessed" you OLD BUZZARD!!! (He lived well in to his nineties.) Mr. E________ in a candid moment commented on a book I was reading at the time namely, "I am Legend" by Richard Matheson - "Well, this sounds pretty exciting, but you better get accustomed to reading "boring books" if you are going to college!!" Incidentally, a friend reported to me that in the holdings of an Estate Sale held at my Mentor's former home after his passing was a Doc Savage novel. A possibly spurious report since so far it has not been confirmed... One afternoon in class Mr. E_______ stood at the head of the class and in what appeared to be an epiphany he pressed the end of one temple support of his glasses against his lips and looked out a great window of the classroom into a solid brick wall of an adjacent building. Then, with a far away look he intoned: "When you are young you have dreams.....Then time passes and you wonder where your dreams have gone....." Reflecting on this statement in view of the comments made above about the fictional nature of "great literature," who was obsessed with "weird, fantastic stories"? Incidentally, Mr. E_____ thoroughly enjoyed Sheridan's 1775 vintage play, "The Rivals" where during class readings I observed his barely concealed mirth when Mrs. Malaprop came through with a "real zinger"!!! We students, however, reacted with a cavernous, "Yawn!!!" . In contrast, I spied Mr. E____ cast a grimace of disdain at the screen, when at a local movie house the audience howled in delight at the antics of the then popular Mr. Magoo.

Why did he not take the prophetic advice of the Shades of Literature when they advised:

Sophocles (speaking through Odysseus) : " I pity him in his misery for all that he is my foe, for he is bound fast to a dread doom . I think of my own lot no less than his. For I see that we are but phantoms, all we who live, or fleeting shadows..."

Yeats; "Come away , O Human child! To the waters and the wild with a faery hand in hand, for the the world's more full of weeping than you can understand...."

Poe: "All we see or seem is but a dream within a dream...."

Shelley (speaking through Ozymandias): "Look at my works, ye Mighty, and despair!"

Numerous other similar quotes could have been included, but these seem sufficient to compose a "weird, fantastic" vision of reality.

For all its lack of "literary value" THE MAGIC ISLAND has everything you might want from great writing except long paragraphs replete with unnecessary detailed descriptions, scientifically invalid facts, long passages of stream of consciousness ruminations, endless conversation and prosaic exploits of ordinary humans!!! Who needs these anyway???? Let's get down to the interesting stuff that any FANATIC is looking for!!! And best of all Kenneth Robeson will never be included in Hornstein - Percy - and Brown's "Reader's Companion to World literature (Guide to Immortal Masterpieces)" (Mentor Book, 1984). Wow!! What good does that do anyway? Make you want to wear a scratchy, tweed suit??? If Dostoevsky had written weird, pulp fiction (as of my reading I am not sure he didn't) Raskolnikov would not have borrowed an axe, spent a lengthy Siberian sojourn, and sweet, little Alyona Ivanova could have retired to a villa in the south of France. And, if Dickens had done the same, would he have needed to ridicule school officials endlessly in Oliver Twist? I could do that for him now. (More effectively? ) What the hey?! Would Leonard Zelig, and others like him have been haunted throughout life simply because they never read "Moby Dick" including its endless exposition on cetology and monomania? Psst! Psst! (Whisper...Whisper) In an introductory literature class in college we were to read the aforementioned novel. I read the Classic Comic instead and got an "A" on the test!!! I love it when that happens!!! Let's get serious here!! If High School students read more pulp fiction they would never have to say, "I haven't read anything since the first grade!!" The "Magic Island" has everything a budding Literaturist could need and more (EVEN an "A" on a book report). And, they have the wonderful property of "not being one page longer" than necessary as M. Twain once quipped. The only one who gets that kind of a grade reading literature is that funny looking kid who sat in the corner over there. He told me he has all eighty odd Dr. Savage novels and they were his favorites. So there!!!

Speaking of boring books, as a college freshmen I was saddled with reading "Crime and Punishment"!!!!! There I sat
on my sagging bunk in a dimly light dormitory hovel - cinder block walls and all, holding this turgid tome in my skinny fingers...... reading about Raskolnikov and his twisted views of reality. My friend at lunch told be he had to read "The Possessed" by the same author. This kid always developed a weird gleam in his eyes when he recounted for me the endless pages of dialog where wretches (all sharing that "Lean and hungry look" not unlike Cassius) ranted regarding "NIHILISM," "IDEALISM" "SUPER MEN" and the like. - At such times his eyes fairly glowed with fascination. (Did I use 'fair' as an adverb? How many days in Hell is that worth?} That kid was gone at the end of the first semester (needless to say). After my reading I avoided splitting firewood at home on Christmas Vacation. My father thought college had made me "effete." Dad also bought me for Christmas a fancy watch from a local Pawn Shop. NO THANKS!!

Well, if you still do not get what I am raving about, go on Amazon and buy a used copy of "The Magic Island" !!!
You will not regret it!!! Scout's Honor!!! (I was a Boy Scout for about three days, so I know about such things)!! Incidentally, if you see Dominic, thank him for me!!

"The works of the great poets have never been read by mankind, for only great poets can read them. (..........) But of reading as a noble intellectual enterprise they know little or nothing; yet this only is reading, in a high sense, not that which lulls us as a luxury and suffers the nobler faculties to sleep the while, but what we have to stand on tip-toe to read and devote our most alert and wakeful hours to."

In view of the analysis of the "noble intellectual enterprise" Thoreau might revise his admonition.

All said, I much admired Mr. E_____. For all my satire I must admit that he influenced me more that any other teacher. I did not become a literaturist, but he provided a wider lens through which to view reality. And therefore provided me with the basis of a better life. Included above I am sure are some punctuation errors (HORRORS!!!) and some ungrammatical formulations (GASP!!!).....I hope he would not have been too deeply wounded by my report to his class.

Your Old Buddy, Whizbang!!!
top_rant.title: A TREASURE!!!

top_rant.text:
I’ve read this collection three times now, and I feel like I’m surprised at the quality each time. Ditko is a great artist, and though I’m more of a Romita fan, he brings a lot of energy to each issue.

Amazing Fantasy #15 ⧫ 5 Stars
“Spider-Man!”
Admittedly, it’s quick, but it lays out Spider-Man’s origin, and it isn’t afraid to make him unsympathetic and somewhat petty, which was revolutionary for the time. Dikto’s art is expressive, though it lacks backgrounds. However, I’m still going to give it five stars.

Amazing Spider-Man #1
“Spider-Man: Freak! Public Menace!” ⧫ 4.5 Stars
This issue focuses more on Spider-man’s human problems and unconventional hero status, still no supervillains! It continues Peter’s money troubles and complicates them with the addition of J. Jonah Jameson. This has some really amazing shots of Spider-man saving the shuttle. I actually think its weakness might be its brevity. The beginning and end are a little crunched.
“Spider-Man vs. the Chameleon!” ⧫ 5 Stars
This issue has both a great villain and great cameos, though seeing Ditko’s version of the Fantastic Four is a little jarring for me. I love that Peter thinks of becoming a member of the Fantastic Four (that will take awhile), and their fight is really fun. Spider sense is introduced rather inconsistently, and people calling Spidey through his spidey sense is rather dumb, but I’ll give it a pass. The Chameleon fight section is also really well thought out. Both the Chameleon and Peter have an intelligent battle of minds. This is everything I want in a Spider-man story.

Amazing Spider-Man #2
“Duel to the Death with the Vulture!” ⧫ 4.5 Stars
I’d argue that the Vulture is Spider-man’s first supervillain. The Chameleon is more of a super-spy. The vulture fight is really fun and creative with the water tower scene being the first example of Spider-Man having to overcome a predicament that his powers might not cover. The end is a little out of nowhere, but the rest is great!
“The Uncanny Threat of the Terrible Tinkerer!” ⧫ 2.5 Stars
Ah, the first relative dud. Even Stan Lee admits that aliens and Spider-man don’t really mix, especially not this early. It’s nice to see the sciency side of Spider-man, but the Tinkerer and his plan aren’t great. The aliens even get retconned out in the 70s

Amazing Spider-Man #3 ⧫ 5 Stars
“Spider-Man versus Doctor Octopus, the Strangest Foe of All Time…”
So, this is essentially a continuation and expansion of the elements from the Chameleon story in issue 1. Doctor Octopus is my pick for Spider-man’s greatest villain (Sorry—not sorry—Goblin fans), and his first issue is awesome. Spider-man’s defeat and demoralization are natural outcroppings of his youth. It also leads into an excellent conclusion where Spider-man uses what he’s learned to win. This issue also officially starts the Human Torch and Spider-man friendship that is one of my favorite relationships in comics. This is a great comic and an essential Marvel issue.

Amazing Spider-Man #4 ⧫ 5 Stars
“Nothing Can Stop... the Sandman!”
Two in a row for powerhouse superhero introductions! This is the first story to blend everything: issues with the police, the difficulties of crime fighting, J. Jonah Jameson, the Parker money troubles, school problems, and a supervillain. It also does it in a way that’s both fun, Peter faking the Sandman photos, and sad, missing a date with Liz. It’s also got the first appearance of the great Betty Brant! All this and the fun fight against Sandman in some excellent Dikto panels.

Strange Tales Annual #2 ⧫ 3 Stars
“On the Trail of the Amazing Spider-Man!”
The Human Torch was at the height of his popularity here, so he had a cash-grab spin-off in Strange Tales. This is one of the run’s better outings as the Torch and Spidey relationship continues here, and they establish their meeting place atop the Statue of Liberty in this issue. The highlight is their fight, a staple of superhero misunderstandings, though there is some silliness with the Torch’s hotheadedness (heh) the silly flame images (a power that doesn’t last), and the contrived ice web. The Fox and his gang are the usual uninspired villains that would show up in Strange Tales (Note: This is not the much more fun Fox, also a master thief, who would show up in the 80s). It’s also fun to see Kirby drawing and Ditko inking. It seems a bit rushed, but I enjoyed it. There’s too much fun here for me to really hate this.

Fantastic Four Annual #1 ⧫ 4 Stars
“The Fabulous Fantastic Four Meet Spider-man!”
This is essentially a longer version of the fight scene from issue 1. The additions are fun, but it’s kind of unnecessary.

Amazing Spider-Man #5 ⧫ 3.5 Stars
“Marked for Destruction by Dr. Doom!”
Spider-man doesn’t remember when Chameleon summoned him with his spider sense? Stan Lee’s memory was pretty bad. Obligatory issue 5 Doctor Doom cameo was a thing then, and Ditko’s Doom looks weird, but the fight is fun, if inconsequential. Doom’s plan is a little weird, but he’s properly menacing, plus bonus points for the Doombot. It continues Peter’s school stuff, but the main new thing is that Spider-man is on the road to his first love interest.

Amazing Spider-Man #6 ⧫ 4 Stars
“Face-to-Face with... the Lizard!”
It’s a fun adventure story with a lot of interaction between Jonah and Peter. Lizard stories are all a little samey, and this is the archetype. The lizard is a neat foe, though his motivations are less than clear. It’s good spider fun!

Amazing Spider-Man #7 ⧫ 4.5 Stars
“The Return of the Vulture”
Am I a Vulture fan? I feel like the Vulture as “old man with wings” has overshadowed how crafty and swift he used to be. The battle in the Daily Bugle office really makes this issue with the ending being a particular fave of mine because Betty Brant is awesome.

Amazing Spider-Man #8
“The Terrible Threat of The Living Brain!” ⧫ 3 Stars
Ah, the Living Brain. He’s a fun bit of kitsch in a really silly story. The Flash Thomson vs. Peter Parker aspect is way more interesting, but the rest is rather meh.
“Spider-Man Tackles the Torch!” ⧫ 3.5 Stars
Speaking of versus battles! It’s a fun fight-for-no-reason issue with some wacky early torch flame constructs. It’s fine fluff.

Amazing Spider-Man #9 ⧫ 4 Stars
“The Man Called Electro!”
Electro is a cool villain and a real threat for Spider-Man. The subplot of May’s illness is good, though Betty and Peter’s rift seems somewhat forced. We’ll get her history later, but it’s still not my favorite. Also, much like the first Vulture defeat, Electro’s end comes out of nowhere. However, I feel like that last panel really works for me and the action is fun, so I’m willing to forgive quite a bit.

Amazing Spider-Man #10 ⧫ 3 Stars
“The Enforcers!”
A rushed gangster mystery story that doesn’t really deliver on much. The Big Man is much better used later on. I think the Enforcers are fine, but they are a rather silly remnant that probably seemed dated even in the early sixties. Betty’s past catches up with her, and I like her leaving to deal with it all on her own, but the last panel is not great.
