Amazing Spider-Man #11 ⧫ 3 Stars
“Turning Point”
I like the stuff between Betty and Peter, but the way Betty acts about Spider-Man seems cliche, even for grief. There are some important moments, but even those are more stereotypical than they need to be. Also, Doc Ock seems somewhat more generic and creepy here, like some of the stereotypical one-off gangster Blackie Glaxton rubbed off on him. The fight on the boat is fun, but the rest, enh. Better Doc Ock next!

Amazing Spider-Man #12 ⧫ 4.5 Stars
“Unmasked By Doctor Octopus!”
Doctor Octopus goes berserk and Spider-Man is unmasked! This unmasking is a seminal moment that is referenced quite often the future. So much so, that I forgot some of the funny stuff that happens in this issue. Betty frees herself! Progressive stuff for the 60s. Also, she gets the classic Betty Brant haircut that I love. The circus animal fights and Doc Ock chase are neat if a little nonsensical. However, the climax in the burning building is pretty great. Even Doctor Octopus’ attitude is better with his obsession and denial feeling much more threatening. This story is more of a real turning point in the series.

Amazing Spider-Man #13 ⧫ 3.5 Stars
“The Menace of... Mysterio!”
So, I like Mysterio, but this issue seems like a lot of repeated themes. Spider-Imposter stealing things, Betty frustratingly worrying, and J. Jonah Jameson being webbed at the end, Even Mysterio seems to mostly just have smoke. He does a few other things, but that’s all to confound Spidey before the smoke. The movie studio fight is fun, and Mysterio posing as a hero is nice, but it just leads to very samey Jonah stuff. It’s not a bad issue, this is where the series starts to spin its wheels sometimes.

Amazing Spider-Man #14 ⧫ 2.5 Stars
“The Grotesque Adventure of the Green Goblin”
Full disclosure, I am not a Green Goblin fan. I think he’s incredibly overrated, and I kind of love that his first adventure is incredibly goofy, rather nonsensical, and very silly. The Goblin has Spider-Man go to two different and incredibly far away states… to surprise attack him. Also, Namor did this back in Fantastic Four Annual #1! It seems more like an excuse to pit Spider-Man against the Hulk, so those pages are actually fun and full of life. Unfortunately, the rest of it isn’t great.

Amazing Spider-Man #15 ⧫ 4 Stars
“Kraven the Hunter!”
The return of the Chameleon and the introduction of Kraven provide a different spin on the villain for hire. Much like the Lizard, Kraven stories tend to be somewhat similar, but his first outing is a good example, especially with the trickery in their final fight. Bringing this issue down is Betty and Liz’s first meeting, which does not bring out my favorite side of Betty, but at least Liz’s doting and Peter’s befuddlement play into it this time. J. Jonah Jameson actually gets to be more of a reliable newspaper publisher with this issue when he notes some potential issues with Kraven. Jonah still roots for Kraven, but at least it shows he’s not a complete nut. Also, this is the first mention of the nice girl who would become Mary Jane Watson. All in all, it’s a classic issue with a cool ground-based final fight.

*Reading Order Note: Lists often put Amazing Spider-Man #16 before Annual #1*

Amazing Spider-Man Annual #1 ⧫ 5 Stars
“The Sinister Six”
This is just a fantastic issue. It’s essentially everything a person needs to know about Spider-Man in one marathon bout. Even the blatant ads for the other Marvel comics are fairly well integrated. The card thing is a bit wonky, but honestly, I forgive it. It’s rightly remembered as a great issue, and it’s one of the best Lee & Ditko.

Amazing Spider-Man #16 ⧫ 3.5 Stars
“Duel with Daredevil”
I really like the stuff with Daredevil, and Ditko draws the character well. 60s Matt Murdock is far more fun than his post-80s counterpart. Unfortunately, there’s some badly written Betty this issue, and the Ringmaster isn’t the most inspiring villain, but I think it’s good enough.

Amazing Spider-Man #17 ⧫ 4.5 Stars
“The Return of the Green Goblin!”
Okay, I gave the Goblin some crap about his first appearance, but I genuinely think his second is great. Not necessarily for him, but because of the unique structure, ending, and fallout in this issue and beyond. Also, it’s got some good use of the Human Torch as a guest star, and I really like the respect building between Spidey and the Torch. Honestly, the only problems I have are the continued poor handling of Liz and Betty.

Amazing Spider-Man #18 ⧫ 4.5 Stars
“The End of Spider-Man!”
No fights needed, only responsibility. It’s a pure character and consequences issue, and I am here for it.

Amazing Spider-Man #19 ⧫ 4 Stars
“Spidey Strikes Back!”
A strong conclusion to a great trilogy. It’s not quite as good as the first two, but it wraps up everything with the Sandman, Enforcers, and of course, J. Jonah Jameson. It also features the first appearance of Ned Leeds who is fine here, but this will cause Peter to get frustratingly jealous soon. Also, Spidey pays the Human Torch back for the help in issue 17.

Amazing Spider-Man #20 ⧫ 2.5 Stars
“The Coming of the Scorpion! Or: Spidey Battles Scorpey!”
So, I think the Scorpion is fine, but his first appearance is pretty wonky. It feels both very stock and far too fast. There is some very convenient non recreatable mad science where the Scorpion is created in a few hours while Peter and Betty see Ned Leeds off? Yeah, that would have been cliche in the 1940s. Also, jealous Peter begins here… However, the Scorpion fight is pretty neat, and the Jonah heelturn is fairly well handled.

Amazing Spider-Man #21 ⧫ 2 Stars
“Where flies the Beetle...!”
It’s all jealousy all the time with another Strange Tales villain with a Torch guest appearance. Now, I’m a Spidey/Torch fan, but this is pretty bad. Dorrie Evans is not great, and this is basically superhero rom com antics. There’s a much better follow up in Untold Tales of Spider-Man Annual ‘96.

Amazing Spider-Man #22 ⧫ 1.5 Stars
“Preeeeeesenting...The Clown, and the Masters of Menace!”
Oh, Circus of Crime, why? More jealous Peter and Betty here with some Peter being befuddled by Princess Python being a girl. It’s all aged worse than the rest of the series. Literally, the best thing is the inside joke about Steve Ditko being unable to draw feet on page 5.

Amazing Spider-Man #23 ⧫ 4 Stars
“The Goblin and the Gangsters”
Honestly, this is a solid issue. Now I’ve hated a Torch issue and liked a Goblin issue! What is happening? This episode has a little of the jealousy plague, but the Goblin’s plan is great as is Spider-Man’s call to Aunt May. This is the kind of Gobby I like.

Amazing Spider-Man #24 ⧫ 3 Stars
“Spider-Man Goes Mad!”
Honestly, I like the switch up of our mystery villain’s plan, but it is also rather silly and requires a ton of work. Plus, most of the background character stuff involves jealousy.

Amazing Spider-Man #25 ⧫ 2.5 Stars
“Captured by J.Jonah Jameson!”
This is a comedy issue with the only sane one being Betty, astonishingly. It seems like J.J.J. forgets what happened with the Scorpion at the slightest provocation. I guess I also really dislike the robot, which will eventually become the first of the Spider Slayer line. Also, this has the famous faceless on-panel appearance of Mary Jane where she looks like a flower is eating her face. Basically, many things bolster this fairly crappy issue.

Amazing Spider-Man #26 ⧫ 4 Stars
“The Man in the Crime-Master's Mask!”
Whereas the previous story redid another issue badly, this issue is essentially the new and improved Amazing Spider-Man #10. I think this issue uses both the Goblin and Foswell’s mysteries to craft a new and impressive story. It helps that the Crime Master is a rather cool character that seems like he stepped from the pulp era. Also, it’s a two parter that has a really good cliffhanger, even the fact that Spider-Man lost his suit plays into this.

Amazing Spider-Man #27 ⧫ 4 Stars
“Bring Back My Goblin to Me!”
And, the conclusion is strong! It continues playing with reader expectations, and aside from a little narrative convenience, sets up a new Daily Bugle status quo nicely.

Amazing Spider-Man #28 ⧫ 3 Stars
“The Menace of the Molten Man!”
The best part of this issue is the cover. The Molten Man will more interesting much later, but right now, he’s just a hood. The Liz Allan sections humanize her right as she leaves the book for awhile, aside from a minor appearance in a few issues. The graduation is fine, but brief. It’s important, but not really for the villain stuff.

Amazing Spider-Man Annual #2 ⧫ 3 Stars
“The Wondrous World of Dr. Strange”
It’s different in terms of Spider-Man, but pretty standard in terms of Doctor Strange.

Amazing Spider-Man #29 ⧫ 2.5 Stars
“Never Step on A Scorpion!”
Peter is a jerk as Ned Leeds returns. The Scorpion is back due to stupid excuse, and while his fight is good, it’s nothing really new.

Amazing Spider-Man #30 ⧫ 3.5 Stars
“The Claws of the Cat!”
This issue has a lot of good Flash, Liz, and Betty stuff, though Peter continues to be kind of a creep. He has his reasons, but still... I’ve bemoaned some of the melodrama, but the Betty-Peter breakup is pretty intense and more real than many of their interactions. Also, love that last panel.

Amazing Spider-Man #31 ⧫ 3.5 Stars
“If This Be My Destiny...!”
The beginning of the Master Planner Saga! It’s also the first Empire State University story, but Gwen and Harry are kind of jerks in their first appearances. This isn’t helped by Flash, and I can’t help but long for the time when they will all be friends. The continuation of the outsider stuff from high school is out of place because college is very different. I think the Master Planner arc starts of well as Peter’s life spins out of control.

Amazing Spider-Man #32 ⧫ 4.5 Stars
“Man on a Rampage!”
I’m always a fan of Peter being desperate, and this issue ends on one of the key moments in Spider-history. Anyone reading it for the first time will probably find it familiar, given that it is often repeated. Much like the Crime Master two-parter, it does a good job of using all the elements of Spider-Man’s life to drive the tension. I do wish the Master Planner’s identity had been revealed with more gravitas, but honestly, I can’t complain.

Amazing Spider-Man #33 ⧫ 5 Stars
“The Final Chapter!”
Desperation, struggle, perseverance. This issue is rightly remembered as one of the greats. There are some frustrating Betty Brant panels, but they’re actually mostly fine. This is the last great Lee-Ditko collaboration.

Amazing Spider-Man #34 ⧫ 2.5 Stars
“The Thrill of the Hunt”
Betty Brant stuff bookends the issue, though it’s not really relevant. I actually like the dream opening. Then, Kraven uses Chameleon tactics, which will have more ramifications in the 80s; otherwise, it’s mostly stuff we’ve seen.

Amazing Spider-Man #35 ⧫ 2.5 Stars
“The Molten Man Regrets...!”
One of the most skippable issues. Cool Ditko art, but the story is just kind of nothing.

Amazing Spider-Man #36 ⧫ 2 Stars
“When Falls the Meteor!”
The looter continues the generic Science Fiction villain theme while Gwen re-enacts the early Peter Parker coward comments! It’s probably the third worst issue in this collection after the Beetle issue and second Circus of Crime appearance.

Amazing Spider-Man #37 ⧫ 3 Stars
“Once Upon a Time, There Was a Robot...!”
This issue is boosted by some solid Foswell stuff and the first (named) appearance of Norman Osborn! Otherwise, the robots are somewhat disappointing, and the Gwen stuff feels like more retread.

Amazing Spider-Man #38 ⧫ 2 Stars
“Just a Guy Named Joe!”
The end of an era. Ditko leaves after this issue. Unfortunately, this is kind of a nothing issue. It’s status quo for the college scenes, and the Joe stuff is very silly and standard. I like to pretend the Lee/Ditko run ended at issue 33.

Ditko and Lee created many of the classic villains, and this collection contains many of them. While the collection tapers off some, the art remains great throughout. There are some heightened emotions and unfortunate sexism of the time, but I still feel like these issues get at some vital parts of being a teenage outsider.
top_rant.title: Original Spider-Man Run Is Still Fun!

top_rant.text:
Angels' Flight by Nalini Singh
(four novellas featuring Guild Hunter characters)
Rating: 5/5 stars
Source: Purchase

As much as I love the Guild Hunter series one would think I would have known about this collection of novellas. Alas, I (thankfully!) stumbled upon it and devoured all four stories almost instantly! Included in this collection are the titles Angels' Pawn, Angels' Judgment, Angel's Wolf, and Angels' Dance and for lovers of the series, these short stories are excellent companions to the series. Each novella gives the reader an incredible amount of background information and a fully developed story about characters who will likely never be big enough in the Guild Hunter world to warrant their own full-length novel.

Angels' Pawn features Ashwini, the semi-nuts Guild Hunter and Janvier, the three-hundred year old vampire Ashwini has hunted three times. Sounds like she's not very good at her job, huh? While Ashwini is used to working alone and considered one of the best among the Hunter community, even she knows her limits. Ashwini (and the Guild) have been contracted to "rescue" a kidnapped vampire. The mission will take the Guild Hunter into the heart of the South where vampire and angel politics are as tricky and unpredictable as quicksand. Ashwini knows she must tread lightly if she wants to come out alive so enlisting Janvier's help seems the most logical choice given the nature of the contract.

Janvier and Ashwini have a bizarre relationship that involves a lot of chasing, tracking, and hunting though never actually catching. As we discover in Angels' Pawn the hunting, chasing, and tracking are more sport and entertainment than business for the two. Because of their past associations with one another there is respect and definite attraction toward one another which leads Janvier to immediately agreeing to help Ashwini. Though the two know they will be successful in their attempt to "rescue" the "kidnapped" vampire they also fully appreciate the inherent danger involved. Though all the plotting, scheming, and killing we get to see Ashwini shine and Janvier play a far more serious role than we are used to from him. We learn more about each character's background, their motivations and desires as well as their intentions toward one another. Janvier can be serious and deadly but he is also charming, playful, and sensual. Ashwini is lethal, focused, and driven but also beautiful and sexy. Her troubled background adds an edge of wariness that Janvier is committed to overcoming though he knows this will take time.

I very much enjoyed the deeper look into these two characters: Janvier is a delightful rogue and Ashwini is serious beauty with a dark past she is working hard to overcome. While I don't think these two will ever warrant their own book I do think we have not seen the last of them in the Guild Hunter world.

Angels' Judgment: novella number two is, for all intents and purposes a prequel to the Guild Hunter series. If you're a fan of Guild Director Sara, then this short story is absolutely for you! Angels' Judgment details one of Sara's last hunts before becoming the Guild's Director; this is also the time she meets her future husband, Deacon.

The meeting of Sara and Deacon is anything but pleasant - there is a psychopath on the loose knocking off vampires. The real problem? The psychopath is likely a Hunter or a crazy bastard imitating a Hunter which is why Deacon has been called. Known among the Hunter community as the Slayer, Deacon is a man of legend, the one called in to hunt and stop rogue Guild Hunters. Few have ever met the Slayer and being in his presence does little to calm Sara's feelings about this case; the potential for taking out one of their own is high and something no member of Hunter community wants to be involved in. Sara is, however a professional and with Deacon's help she will do what must be done to bring the killer to justice. Because of the length of this read, the action is fast, furious and seriously intense. It is a wild ride from start to finish and even has some naughty bits thrown in to spice things up 

I loved this particular novella! I have always been fond of Sara in the regular series as she is practical, level-headed, loyal, and absolutely perfect as Guild Director. Quite simply, Sara doesn't take crap off anyone! But, we have always only been given a limited amount of information about her; Sara has been a part of the plot as she relates to Elena and Raphael more than anything else. Angels' Judgment highlights not only Sara's talents as a Guild Hunter but Deacon's talents as both a Hunter and the Slayer. The background story for both Sara and Deacon (especially Deacon!) is very interesting and kept me turning pages at a lightning pace. Honestly, I didn't want this particular story to end because Deacon is not only a sweet man but dead sexy and deadly with his weapons. Oh, and he is the mastermind behind all the amazing weapons the Guild Hunters use - seriously, can this man get any hotter?  Deacon is instrumental in helping Sara take down the psychopath and vice versa. You see immediately how good the two Hunters are together and how they perfectly balance and complement one another. For fans of Sara and Deacon, this one is a must-read.

Novella number three is Angel's Wolf and is the story of Noel and Nimra. Fans of the series may recall Noel as the poor bastard who was beaten to within an inch of his vampiric life and left for dead within the confines of the Refuge. Noel has been with Raphael for more than 200 years choosing to serve the archangel following the expiration of his 100 year Contract. Noel's rise within Raphael's ranks of trusted companions was nothing short of meteoric right up to the moment he was left for dead. Though fully healed and back in service, Noel feels he is being punished when Raphael sends him to the court of Nimra in the deep South. Despite his intelligence and intuition, even Noel is unprepared for what he finds in Nimra's court.

Nimara is a 500+ year old angel who controls a section of the deep South on Raphael's behalf. Though beautiful beyond belief and surprisingly human (she loves cats!!) given her age, Nimra has a nasty, nasty, nasty reputation and very few, even among the very old, have ever dared go up against her. By far, Nimara has one of the coolest powers found among any of the angels and/or archangels in the Guild Hunter world. And no, I will not tell you what that power is  Though generally absolutely capable of defending her court and her territory Nimra has discovered one thing she cannot defend against, an unknown assassin hell-bent on taking her out.

Disguised as Nimra's new bedroom toy, Noel is given free rein to wander the court in an effort to determine who the would-be assassin is. Loose lips, gossip and being thought of as nothing more than a harmless and temporary sex toy allows Noel to quickly determine who the perpetrator is. Unfortunately the identity of the traitor is devastating for Nimra. The intensity of the situation draws Noel and Nimra together and they quickly discover they can take comfort and strength from one another without sacrificing who they are as individuals. In fact, the two understand almost immediately what a good and strong pair they are; they both balance and complement one another. Nimra's strength and über-powerful abilities are tempered by her humanity making her one of the most endearing of all of Singh's characters. Though I am fairly certain Nimra and Noel's story is complete, I must say it is sad to see them tucked away in the deep South and not making more appearances in the Guild Hunter series.

The fourth and final novella in Angels' Flight is Angels' Dance, the story of Jessamy and Galen. Jessamy is more than 2,500 years and beloved and thoroughly well-respected throughout the angelic community. Jessamy serves the angelic community as both teacher to the young and historian - there is nothing she doesn't know about angelic history making her the perfect teacher for the breed's young. Jessamy is patient, kind, wise, and trusted absolutely by most of the Cadre of Ten. On the outside Jessamy is calm, cool, and collected but for most of her 2,500 years she has held herself apart from everyone because of her deformity - Jessamy is an angel who cannot fly. For Jessamy her inability to fly is a life-sentence: she cannot leave the safety of Refuge; she cannot have children for fear of passing on her deformity and; she certainly cannot fall in love as her deformity would be both a hindrance and embarrassment to her mate.

Galen is an oddity in the angelic world for he has chosen to transfer from Titus's court in order to become the weapon's master and a member of Raphael's Seven. Though new to the Refuge, Galen is not new to weapons work and training and quickly earns respect among other members of the Seven. When Jessamy's life is threatened Raphael is wicked pissed and Galen is called into action to protect Raphael's former teacher. What takes both Galen and Jessamy by surprise is their response to one another. Though attracted to Galen, Jessamy is certain, based on his brutish nature and lack of manners that the man was raised by wolves. Galen is insta-attracted to Jessamy and can't seem to rein himself in around her.

During the course of their investigations the two learn to respect one another and reevaluate their personal positions concerning attachments and entanglements. While there is a mutual respect and the two can work together, being together is a cat of a different color. Both Galen and Jessamy are outrageously stubborn and this trait keeps them apart far longer than it should but when they do finally let down their respective barriers, the meeting is explosive! Like Sara's story, Jessamy's story is a personal favorite and I was exceedingly glad to find it among the collection that makes up Angels' Flight.

The Bottom Line: Angels' Flight can absolutely be read as a stand-alone but I would highly recommend reading it in addition to the "regular" Guild Hunter series. This collection of novellas is a wonderful enhancement of a series I am already slutty in love with. This book got me so fired up about the Guild Hunter series that after finishing Angels' Flight I immediately went back and re-read the first two books in the series! I can't think of a reason not to recommend this fantastic paranormal series to mature young adults, new adults, and adult readers. I'm not sure why you're still here - you should be buying this series, NOW!!
top_rant.title: Four freakin' awesome reads in one!!

top_rant.text:
***Review posted on The Eater of Books! blog***

The Sea of Tranquility by Katja Millay
Publisher: Atria
Publication Date: June 4, 2013
Rating: 2 stars
Source: eARC from NetGalley

Summary (from Goodreads):

I live in a world without magic or miracles. A place where there are no clairvoyants or shapeshifters, no angels or superhuman boys to save you. A place where people die and music disintegrates and things suck. I am pressed so hard against the earth by the weight of reality that some days I wonder how I am still able to lift my feet to walk.

Full of rage and without a purpose, former pianist Nastya Kashnikov wants two things: to get through high school without anyone discovering her past and to make the boy who took everything from her pay.

All 17 year-old Josh Bennett wants is to build furniture and be left alone, and everyone allows it because it's easier to pretend he doesn't exist. When your name is synonymous with death, everyone tends to give you your space.

Everyone except Nastya, a hot mess of a girl who starts showing up and won't go away until she's insinuated herself into every aspect of his life. The more he gets to know her, the more of a mystery she becomes. As their relationship intensifies and the unanswered questions begin to pile up, he starts to wonder if he may ever learn the secrets she's been hiding or if he even wants to.

The Sea of Tranquility is a slow-building, character-driven romance about a lonely boy, an emotionally fragile girl, and the miracle of second chances.

What I Liked:

When all the hype over another indie-to-traditional publishing novel somewhat died down, I decided to pick this one up and read it. It's wayyyy past the time I needed to read it to submit a review to NetGalley, and wayyyy past the hype of the book, but I am glad that I waited so long to read this book. A lot of times, hype has the opposite effect on me - making me NOT want to read the book (like Defiance, for example). The only difference is, I loved Defiance. I didn't love this book.

The synopsis of this story and all those glowing reviews led me to believe that this book is a must-read, do-not-miss novel, especially since it did well enough that Atria noticed, and bought the rights. For some reason, I was expecting some dynamic, life-changing romance, and an intense plot dealing with Nastya's past.

The plot was not intense, but I can see why everyone said that this book was intense and mesmerizing and whatnot. A lot of the book is Nastya going on tangents about her past, before the accident. We get small hints about what happened that night, but we don't get the full story until close to the end (past the middle of the book). But a lot of the book is Nastya thinking about what used to be.

This book is split into Josh's perspective and Nastya's perspective. So, every other chapter or so, we see the perspective switch. I really liked this aspect of the book. Of course, it solidifies the idea that Josh and Nastya are the love interests and couple of the story, which is a downer because I like to discover the relationship as it goes. Knowing right from the start that they are the ones that will fall in love and whatnot is a tad bit disappointing.

I vastly preferred Josh's point-of-view over Nastya's point-of-view. Josh is not the golden boy of the school, though he seems to be sometimes. He is quiet and intelligent and he works hard. His train of thought is much easier and more enjoyable to follow than Nastya's train of thought.

The story behind this book, Nastya's story, is heart-breaking. I can't say I really enjoyed the story or this book, but what happened to Nastya is awful and extremely sad. Ms. Millay has written a very powerful novel, and I may not love this book, but I see the message behind this book.

What I Did Not Like:

This might come off as a rant of disrespectful - and I'm hoping you readers don't think that. I mean no disrespect towards the author or the publisher or any other readers of this book.

I really did not enjoy this book as much as I hoped. I'm giving it two stars (as opposed to one star) because the story is heart-breaking yet powerful, and I like the overall message of the story. I like the outline or skeleton of the book, but with the "meat" of the story, I had problems.

This book is long. Length never bothers me with books - I love long books. BUT. This book was long, and definitely boring. I got so tired of just reading this book, which almost never happens! Most of this book is Nastya's musings about things before the accident, or Josh brooding over Nastya. But seriously, about 40% of this book - the first 40% of the book - was boring and totally unnecessary.

Ms. Millay uses the first 40% of the book to introduce us to Josh and Nastya and the situation of Nastya. But I feel like 40% of an already long book is overkill. Not even half of the text in the first half of the book is necessary for readers to get a feel for Nastya's world. I was so bored out of my mind, I almost DNF'd this book. I couldn't see the appeal, but I kept reading, hoping to find it.

Honestly, I never did. I still don't see why everyone went OMG over this book. Besides the fact that overall, the book was boring, and the first half of the book is really slow and REALLY boring, there is the fact that I absolutely hated Nastya.

Okay, don't get me wrong. I understand the psychology of trauma and unforeseen traumatic events and whatnot. But there were so many things that Nastya did that made me think, is she really suffering, or is she using her tragic situation as an excuse? To me, it felt like a bunch of excuses (i.e., bulls***). So, Nastya dresses like a complete whore because she wants people to think she's a whore? Her reasoning is that she knows people will talk about her, so why not give them an excuse?

WHAT?! Really?! Is that what you think?! Talk about flawed logic. You're going to wear skin-tight, low-cut shirts and tiny, itty bitty skirts, and HIGH-HEELS to school and disgusting "black s***", because you want people to talk about you as you are a whore, and not for some other reason? REALLY?! I'm a teen (not a messed-up teen, but a teen), and there is NO WAY I would ruin my public image like that. That's disgusting.

And how about what happened with Josh, when you-know-what finally happened? She leaves because she just wanted him to ruin her completely?! What does that even mean?! He didn't ruin you if it was consensual, and you're f***ing stupid if you use someone you DO care about just for sex, so you can FEEL like a whore. Or whatever her ridiculous logic was. I'm already convinced that Nastya left her brain somewhere.

Again, I understand the psychological damage that comes from traumatic situations. But I don't think Nastya acted out of her pain and suffering. She's full of s*** and makes excuses because she doesn't want to face the inevitable truth that terrible things in life happen, even to the best of us.

I'm sorry, but I just don't like Nastya. She and I would not get along if she were a real person, because I would be calling her out on her s*** every five seconds. All of my friends know that I am honest and forthright and I don't spare feelings when it comes to bulls***. Nastya needs a darn good therapist that will kick her in the pants every time she feels sorry for herself.

So, this entire boring book was spent on babying Nastya and trying to help her heal from the incident that we finally understand by the end of the book. I love the message of the story, and the author's use of a traumatic situation in this novel, but seriously?! This whole, LONG, boring book is spent trying to make Nastya feel better.

I thought I knew what to expect with this one, but I did NOT get what I expected. I expected some earth-shattering romance... nope. The romance was kind of bland. I can tell that Josh really like Nastya (for whatever reason), but I'm not convinced on her end. So, I hated the romance in this book as well.

So... yeah. I'm not going to say anymore because now I'm all fired up and I've decided that this book was not for me.

Would I Recommend It:

No, I would not. Though I think several thousand people would.

Rating:

2 stars (I changed it from 3 stars to 2 stars, because I am all about rounding 2.5 stars down when I dislike the book)
top_rant.title: Review for The Sea of Tranquility by Katja Millay

top_rant.text:
[EDITED] This review has been edited in light of A. Rachamim's idiotic response. So I have refined this review of "Rats to Riches" to be more specific and detailed.

The first thing to note about this book is the title... "Rats to Riches" by Usher Morgan. This is an excellent title to this book as it bears the evidence of who Mr. Morgan is and how he operates.

To start with, if you have seen his YouTube videos for this book, young Mr. Morgan states that he will have 20 videos that are audio versions of this book. In them you hear over and over of how to get the book for totally free. This is his first con. By going to his one website, "Library Tales Publishing" (which is yet another insight to Mr. Morgan's operation" you will find that you actually have to pay for the book. The cost is not much but that is still considered false marketing under U.S. consumer laws.

Additionally the book is a nothing more of a crafty "tale" of how Usher Morgan came to the U.S. and merely plagiarized a good number of well -known and respected authors and started a "virtual" publishing company after totaling failing at becoming a real estate investor. Due to the fact that Morgan is falsely advertising and plagiarizing other authors, I have to give this book the worst possible scoring!

I do find it very humorous and interesting that on the e-book version of the book, Mr. Morgan attempted to place coping restrictions, which is easy to get around and you can get the book absolutely FREE as I did without pirating it. That is totally interesting when in fact Morgan has copied a ton of ideas, content, phrases, concepts, etc., from these other authors but is fearful of someone coping his copied work.

Personally I think the book is poorly written in terms that he jumps around with the so-called factual events of his early childhood. Within the first few chapters he tells of some rat fictional rat story. Perhaps describing himself? Then the book jumps to more of his young adult life of coming to America in hopes of getting rich. He then jumps back to his early childhood again. I find this to be not only confusing to most, but more so that it is evidence that he is inexperienced as an author. Or perhaps the fact that by jumping around you lose sight that he has merely plagiarized other authors and making money selling it to you... the consumer.

As for real estate investing, there is no real help in this book other than the true facts that he doesn't know what he is doing! On page 36 you see where he is in Fort Wayne, Indiana buying real estate, renovating, and then renting them. Based on various dated in various chapters this is between 2004 and 2009. He buys this one property for $7000 and clearly states that the roof is caving in and in major need of renovations... just the way he likes it! As a licensed real estate agent in multiple states and holding a home inspection certification as well as certification as a construction inspector with a degree in contractor construction management, you ARE NOT BUYING MUCH FOR $7000! Clearly this is not the person I would recommend reading his books to learn about real estate.

He mentions Dolf DeRoos and few realize that Robert T. Kiyosaki, the author of the Rich Dad, Poor Dad series, learned real estate investing from Dolf DeRoos. Ironically if you look at Kiyosaki's books and operation, he claims he is an investor. Actually that is a bit deceiving. He is first and foremost AN EDUCATOR JUST LIKE HIS POOR DAD. The money THEN makes from his seminars, board game and books are then used to fund and leverage his real estate investing. Nothing wrong with that, but again just see what is really going on. Kiyosaki partnered with DeRoos to add Real Estate Riches to the Rich Dad Poor Dad series. Google it and see for yourself! I do recommend DeRoos's real estate books or course!
In short Usher Morgan has simply followed suit to of Kiyosaki.

Next Morgan buys a 12 unit apartment building in Henderson, Nevada in May of 2009 (pages 45 - 48) for $597,000. I give Morgan a lot of credit for trying, but this story is excellent evidence that he is/was very inexperienced with buying or flipping real estate. When I go to Vegas I stay in Henderson due to it being so cheap. The last flip I done there was in 2007 and I got out! The few years invested there, I took about $12K and went just over the foothills to Boulder City and learned to fly helicopters. Now if I want to view property or travel I can charter / rent a helicopter... plus use it as a tax write off!

Chapter 6 "The Family Business" (page 81) is also an excellent example of Morgan's constant over exaggerating throughout the book. His parents had a winery in Israel. Young Morgan claims to have been the Vice President but really a Business Development Officer... not a Chief, but merely a development officer. In short merely grunt. His parents paid him only $25,000. Geeze when I was construction Project Manager I was getting $125K plus bonuses and other perks. That was in Vegas and Phoenix before the housing market crash! If Moran was smart he would study the housing starts for the area(s) he was investing in. The point is he over exaggerates!

A few other points are that he really over exaggerates coming back from a failed business attempt of selling painting in Spain and having to work a mere 5 hours for his brother. Also he highly over exaggerates about being homeless for one night in New York. He has little money and on page 136 he talks about not being able to get government help. What a crock of crap! If he would have been smart and while working for his uncle Eli in New York and reporting income, he could have gotten unemployment! Secondly, he is a foreigner which makes him a minority and entitled to all sort of government goodies! Just look at all of the illegal Mexicans who get free health care, equal rights and more!

Let me hit on this a bit more! When I started my construction & excavating business at 23 back in the early 90's I showed myself only working my business for 20 hours a week which enabled me to collect unemployment, and the money I was making in my business. Yes I was reporting ALL income. But under IRS rules within 7 years you can show a loss for 3. See how that works? Additionally, I applied for a small business loan from the Small Business Administration (SBA). To meet the requirements you have to be turned down by 3 conventional banks. I played over achiever and got turned down by 10. Then on paper, I listed my ex as control co-owner (51%) which then made me a minority owned business. We walked into the bank and I got my first government backed micro loan for $35,000 at 3% ARP and 15 years to pay it off! Moreover, we then were able to bid on public sector projects and the got grants! Yes they gave us money to buy equipment, build a new building. What they won't give it for is payroll or operating expenses. Further many national trade publications began writing articles on me and my businesses, and how I used government money to start and fund them!

Now with that being said, look in Morgan's Appendix, again he state there is a cost to taking risk and doing business. Bull Pucky! I just proved it! For additional proof, Moran mentions Donald Trump's book the "The Art of the Deal". I have that book too and all the others he mentions. But the point is that Trump bought his property in Cincinnati, Ohio with money he got from the government the SAME way I did! So when it comes to business who would you seek advice from... Morgan or someone like myself?

Morgan stated that he couldn't get any government help merely because he had two "virtual" Limited Liability Companies. Again that is a bunch of crap from this young con-artist!

Morgan also cites in one section that "this is where most How to... authors leave off" and he picks up the ball to go the extra mile to give the reader the real facts most don't. That is crap too! Page 129 he talks about getting free advisors? Okay where? Who are they? I will tell you! Contact your local S.C.O.R.E. chapter which is short for Senior Core of Retired Executives. They offer free mentoring!

On page 130, Morgan admits to using the internet to download copyrighted business contracts and documents and the added his own clauses to suit his needs. I have to laugh at A. Rachamim's post to my negative review of this book. This is probably Morgan under an alias or someone with vested interest. This is their response... "So let me get this straight? Your giving this book a bad review because you say that Morgan said that the book is free, but it's not free because there is a restriction from copying it and distributing its content? by the way, you're NOT allowed to copy or distribute any part of the book including for educational purposes without permission from the author, I'm sure that the publisher can see this posting and is probably looking into it right now." Okay, stop and think for a moment, why is this so important to this person? Read page 136 and then read this comment again. Morgan also mentions in the book that he keeps an eye on book reviews to see how they are doing. Now wouldn't you agree that this might be Morgan or someone with vested interest? Clearly this person is ignorant not only on copyright laws but how the legal system works here in the U.S.

Lastly on pages 146 to 148, Morgan provides the facts of his operation. I failed at real estate investing... at least at the time. So he turns to plagiarizing authors such as Donald Trump, Anthony Robbins, Dolf DeRoos, Bill Gates, Napoleon Hill, George S. Clason, Robert T. Kiyosaki, and many others. He pays a place to get mail and have a New York based address while living and working out of a New Jersey motel room. While on paper it says he owns Digital Magic Entertainment and Library Tales Publishing, he used a graphic design company out in California, and a real book publishing company called Lightening Source to actually print the books. Hence Morgan is merely a middle man.

Now while having a "virtual office" is not illegal and I have done the same with a couple of my businesses, I oppose this book and Morgan's approach. If you read Trump's book the Art of the Deal (and I do agree with Morgan that is great book regardless of if you love or hate Trump), but Morgan stole Trump's concept of listing diary journal for a week just as Trump did in his book. This is just one example. Read the books of the other authors mentioned here and in Rats to Riches and you will see the proof for yourself!

Now when you go about marketing something is free, then lie and then charge a person, that is dishonest and unethical! And to further deceive consumers who purchase this book into believing that this is his work... I have no respect for people like that! Again I do give him credit for what his is trying to accomplish and he does have a some good points, but just save your time and money and read the actual books of the authors named in his book and here.
top_rant.title: Save your Time & Money! Just another young person with no integrity trying to make it on his own!!!

top_screaming.text:
IN TERMANN'S LOGITUDINAL STUDY BEGUN IN THE 1920s, "ONE HUNDRED STUDIES OF GENIUS" HIS GROUP STUDIED THE NON-ASIAN (EXCLUDED BECAUSE MOST SPOKE POOR ENGLISH AND WOULD DISTORT RESULTS) POPULATION OF CALIFORNIA AND CAME TO A CONCLUSION THAT JEWS HAVE TWICE THE RATE OF GENIUS OF THE GROUP AS A WHOLE AND BLACKS HAVE THE LOWEST IQ LEVEL OF ALL GROUPS STUDIED. THE HIGH SCORES FOR JEWS ARE WITHOUT DOUBT EXPLAINED BY CULTURAL-RELIGIOUS FACTORS. THE LOW SCORES FOR BLACKS ARE NOT SO OBVIOUS.
ABOUT 30 OR 40 YEARS AGO A HARVARD MANAGEMENT PROFESSOR NAMED MCCLELLAN WROTE A PAPER ON WHAT HE CALLED "N-ACHIEVEMENT" (NEED FOR ACHIEVEMENT)IN WHICH AS PART OF HIS STUDY HE HAD PRE-TEEN SCHOOL CHILDREN IN VARIOUS COUNTRIES TAKE AN ARITHMETIC TEST TO ESTABLISH HOW MANY ANSWERS COULD BE PROVIDED TO MATH PROBLEMS WITHIN A GIVEN TIME SPAN. ALL COUNTRIES' CHILDREN SCORED ABOUT THE SAME EXCEPT INDIA. MCCLELLAN PROVIDED AN EXPLANATION THAT A CAST OF HINDUS HEAVILY REPRESENTED IN THE INDIAN GROUP PUT VERY STRONG EMPHASIS UPON TEACHING CHILDREN ARITHMETIC. THIS, TOO, WAS A CASE WHERE CULTURE CAUSED AN ABERRATION.
BLACKS SCORE LOWER NOT ONLY ON SAT AND ACT TESTS FOR COLLEGE ENTRY BUT OTHER TESTS AS WELL SUCH AS THE MCAT (MEDICAL COLLEGE ACCEPTANCE TEST). THE FACT THAT JEWS AND A CASTE OF HINDUS SCORED EXCEPTIONALLY HIGH WITH RESULTS CULTURALLY EXPLAINABLE LEADS ONE TO CONCLUDE THAT THE LOW SCORES FOR BLACKS MAY ALSO BE CULTURALLY INDUCED.
SOME OF THE RESULTS REPORTED IN "THE BELL CURVE" POINT TO A BIOLOGIC EXPLANATION. BUT SOMETHING IS WRONG. FOR INSTANCE IN CASES WHERE BLACK CHILDREN WERE RAISED BY WHITE FAMILIES AND DID POORLY ON TESTS AS A GROUP THE CONCLUSION OF GENETIC CAUSATION MAKES AN IMPLICIT ASSSUMPTION THAT THE EFFECT UPON THE CHILD WOULD RUN PARALLEL AND EQUAL TO A WHITE CHILD ADOPTED BY A WHITE FAMILY. THIS IS DOUBTFUL. IT DOES, OF COURSE, NOT EXCLUDE PEER PRESSURE BY BLACK MEDIA AND OTHER BLACK CHILDREN AND ADULTS TO WHICH THE ADOPTED BLACK CHILD WOULD BE MORE SUSCEPTIBLE THAN AN ADOPTED WHITE CHILD IN A WHITE FAMILY. EQUALLY QUESTIONABLE IS THE CONCLUSION THAT TESTING OF BLACK CHILDREN WHERE THEIR NATURAL FAMILIES WERE WELL-TO-DO SHOWED ACADEMIC RESULTS INFERIOR TO WHITE CHILDREN RAISED IN POVERTY. PEER PRESSURE, BLACK POPULAR FIGURES AND SUCH WOULD PLAY A ROLE IN THIS CASE WHICH WOULD BE DETRIMENTAL IN PART BECAUSE BLACK ENTERTAINMENT IS ADDRESSED TO A POPULAR AUDIENCE, NOT AN ACADEMICALLY ELITE AUDIENCE..
SOME ENVIRONMENTAL INFLUENCES ARE CLEARLY WORKING CONTRARY TO EDUCATION ORIENTED ACCOMPLISHMENT AMONG BLACK CHILDREN AND YOUNG ADULTS, SUCH AS THE INFLUENCE OF HIP-HOP MUSIC AND THE GLORIFICATION OF VIOLANCE ON TV, THE REPETITION OF TRITE PHRASES BY BLACK PERFORMERS AND THE HIGHLY SIMPLIFIED BEAT OF MODERN BLACK MUSIC WHICH NUMB THE UNPROTECTED MIND. THERE IS A CLOSE PARALLEL TO THE MIND-NUMBING EFFECTS OF WARTIME PROPAGANDA FILMS ON UNSOPHISTICATED VIEWERS.
IT WOULD SEEM FROM BLACKS' EXCELLING IN SUCH THINGS AS DANCING AND CERTAIN SPORTS THAT A MENTALITY EMPHASIZING SPATIAL RELATIONSHIPS AS OPPOSED TO NON-SPATIAL THINKING MAY BE INDUCED INTO THE MINDS OF BLACK CHILDREN MORE THAN WHITE OR ASIAN CHILDREN. IT COULD EVEN BE THAT SUCH VARIATION IS PART OF HUMAN INSTINCT'S VARIATION. I DO NOT KNOW.
TAKEN IN ISOLATION "THE BELL CURVE" CERTAINLY POINTS TO A BIOLOGIC BASIS FOR LOW TEST SCORES AMONG BLACKS. THE MEANINGFULNESS OF THE ARGUMENTS FOR BIOLOGIC BASES IS QUESTIONABLE TAKEN IN THE CONTEXT OF LIFE EXPERIENCE AND THE SIMILAR, THOUGH REVERSED, EXPERIENCE OF JEWS AND SOME CASTES OF HINDUS AS WELL AS TO THE GENERAL EXPERIENCE OF CHINESE IN EAST ASIA OUTSIDE CHINA AND IN THE WEST THAT EXPERIENTIAL FACTORS MAY PLAY A MUCH GREATER PART IN BLACK ACADEMIC UNDERACHIEVEMENT AS A GROUP THAN BIOLOGIC FACTORS. AS FAR AS ATTACKS UPON THE STATISTICAL METHODOLOGIES USED BY THE AUTHORS ARE CONCERNED, THEY AR\O\PPEAR UNWARRANTED EXCEPT WHERE THEY SUPPORT THE BIOLOGIC ORIGINS OF BLACK UNDERACHIEVEMENT. THE METHOD OF REACHING CONCLUSIONS, NOT THE STATISTICAL METHODOLOGIES, IS IN DOUBT. NEVERTHELESS, BECAUSE OTHER SOURCES CONFIRM THE BASIC CONCLUSION THAT BLACKS AS A WHOLE ARE DOING ACADEMICALLY LESS WELL THAN FOLKS OF EITHER EUROPEAN OR ASIAN BACKGROUND, THIS CONCLUSION IN "THE BELL CURVE" SEEMS WELL ESTABLISHED.
THERE WILL ALWAYS, AS LONG AS PEOPLE VARY BY RELIGION, CULTURE AND SUCH, BE DIFFERENCES IN IQ AND SIMILAR TEST SCORES. EVERYTHING IN LIFE IS CULTURALLY BIASED. THE ONLY WAY TO KNOW THE NATIVE INTELLECTS OF INDIVIDUALS OR GROUPS IS TO DO SOME SORT OF SCIENCE FICTION IQ TEST WITHIN 5 MINUTES AFTER BIRTH.
AT PRESENT PEOPLE ON BOTH SIDES OF THE NATURE v CULTURE DEBATE ARE IN THE DARK. IT IS OBVIOUS TO EVERYONE THAT BOTH ARE IMKPORTANT. IT IS OBVIOUS THAT READING MAIMONIDES IS GOING TO HAVE AN INFLUENCE DIFFERENT FROM READING COMIC BOOKS. BUT WHO IS TO SAY THE COMIC BOOK READER COULD NOT UNDERSTAND MAIMONIDES AS WELL AS THE OTHER READER HAD HE BEEN INTRODUCED TO MAIMONIDES INSTEAD OF OR AS WELL AS COMIC BOOKS--AND BENEFIT AS MUCH FROM THE THOUGH PROCESSES INTRODUCED BY MAIMONIDES AS ANYONE. OF COURSE PEOPLE BEING PEOPLE, MORE PARENTS ARE GOING TO LET THEIR CHILDREN READ COMIC BOOKS THAN ARE GOING TO LEAD THEIR CHILDREN TO MAIMONIDES--OR SHAKSPEARE OR ARISTOTLE. ALL OF US ARE BORN IGNORANT AND LOTS OF PEOPLE, OUT OF DUTY OR FOR FUN OR PROFIT, TAKE IT UPON THEMSELVES TO KEEP US IGNORANT OR TEACH A LOT OF STUFF THAT AIN'T SO. THEY ARE MUCH EASIER TO FIND THAN PEOPLE WHO REALLY UNDERSTAND AND ARE WILLING TO TEACH. THE MORE RESPECT A KID HAS FOR THESE IGNORANCE MONGERS, THE LOWER IS GOING TO BE HIS ACADEMIC ACOMPLISHMENT.
top_screaming.title: A BOOK WORTH READING

top_screaming.text:
ISRAEL EHRENBERG, WHO WROTE UNDER THE NAME ASHLEY MONTEGU, PRESENTS IN THIS BOOK AN EXTENDED ARGUMENT IN FAVOR OF INTERRACIAL BREEDING. I READ THIS BOOK MANY YEARS AGO WHILE STUDYING GENETICS AND OTHER SCIENCES. I EXPECTED TO FIND MEANINGFUL ARGUMENT; HOWEVER HIS ARGUMENT CONSISTS OF A DISCUSSION OF HYBRID VITALITY. I WAS OF COURSE SOMEWHAT FAMILIAR WITH THE SUBJECT, BUT ONLY APPLIED TO PLANTS. I AM STILL NOT ENTIRELY SURE WHAT HE INTENDED TO TALK ABOUT. THE BREEDS OF DOGS ARE OFTEN LESS HEALTHY THAN A WELL-MIXED MONGREL, BUT THAT IS BECAUSE HUMANS HAVE SELECTED CHARACTERISTICS FOR HUMAN PURPOSES, NOT FOR THE ABILITY OF THE ANIMAL TO SURVIVE IN THE WILD. FIGURING HE COULD NOT BE INTENDING ANYTHING AS OBVIOUSLY INCORRECT AS THAT, I REASONED THAT HE MUST BE TALKING ABOUT WILD TYPES' GREATER VIABILITY RELATIVE TO MUTANTS, WHICH IS A WELL-ESTABLISHED FACT, MOST MUTATIONS BEING HARMFUL, SEMI-LETHAL OR LETHAL. IF A PARTICULAR ANIMAL HAS A MUTATION WHICH IS HARMFUL, IT MAY BE ABLE TO BREED, LIKE SHORT WINGED FRUIT FLIES.
SHOULD THE ANIMAL BE A HUMAN AND THE TRAIT BE HETEROZYGOUS DOMINANT AND THE TRAIT NOT BE SEX LINKED AND THE MATING PARTNER IS HOMOZYGOUS RECESSIVE, ONE IN FOUR OF THE OFFSPRING WILL HAVE THE TRAIT. CONCERNING THAT PARTICULAR TRAIT, THE RACE(S) OF THE PARTNERS WILL HAVE NO IMPACT.THE ONLY FACTOR WHICH WILL BE SIGNIFICANT REGARDING THE TRAIT IS THE DISTRIBUTION OF THE TRAIT IN POTENTIAL MATING PARTNERS. MATING OF AN AFFECTED GROUP WITH A NON-CARRIER GROUP WITHIN THE SAME RACE WILL HAVE THE SAME PROBABILITY OF TRANSMITTAL AS WERE THE MATING GROUP OF ANOTHER RACE. TAKEN OVERALL, THERE IS THE PROBABILITY OF ACCEPTING INTO THE OFFSPRING HARMFUL TRAITS, PROBABLY RECESSIVE, FROM EACH GROUP.
SHOULD THERE EXIST A POPULATION FREE OF ALL HARMFUL TRAITS, DOMINANT OR RECESSIVE, THE BEST THING (LOWEST RISK) WOULD BE TO HAVE INTRA-GROUP BREEDING ONLY. SHOULD THERE BE TWO SUCH POPULATIONS AND THEY INTERBRED THERE WOULD BE, TAKEN INDIVIDUALLY, NO HARM TO THE OFFSPRING. ACCORDING TO ASHLEY MONTAGU'S ARGUMENT, THERE SHOULD BE SOME NET BENEFIT SHOULD THE TWO GROUPS BE OF DIFFERENT RACES AS OPPOSED TO BEING ISOLATED COMMUNITIES OF THE SAME RACE. CARLTON S. COON CLASSIFIED MAN INTO FIVE BASIC RACES (WITH MANY PEOPLE MIXED OF COURSE). THESE RACES HE CALLED: CAUCASOID, MONGOLOID, CONGOID (ALSO CALLED NEGROID), CAPOID (HOTTENTOT BUSHMEN) AND AUSTROLOID. WHETHER THEY ARE ACTUAL PRIMARY RACES IN THE SENSE OF BEING LINKED TO A COMMON PRE-MODERN COMMON ANSESTOR IS A MATTER OF SPECULATION. ONE THING IS CERTAIN--THEY ARE ALL WILD TYPES. EACH HAS PROVED ITSELF CAPABLE OF SURVIVAL OVER TENS OR HUNDREDS OF THOUSANDS OF YEARS. ALL HAVE ABOUT THE SAME NATURAL LIFE SPAN. ALL ARE CAPABLE OF LANGUAGE AND REASONED DECISION MAKING. THERE IS AN INTERESTING ISSUE WHICH A PRIORI SEEMS A POTENTIAL ARGUMENT AGAINST THE BREEDING HE ADVOCATES. EACH RACE HAS EVOLVED TRAITS WHICH ARE BENEFICIAL IN THE CONTEXT OF THE WHOLE BODY OF TRAITS. FOR EXAMPLE SKULL SIZE AND EYE SIZE NEED TO CORRESPOND WITH ONE ANOTHER TO PRODUCE GOOD VISION. TOOTH SIZE MUST CORRESPOND WITH SKULL SIZE TO PRODUCE TEETH THAT FIT CORRECTLY IN THE MOUTH. AS FAR A RACES ARE CONCERNED (OR ISOLATED POPULATIONS WITHIN THE SAME RACE) THE WAY TO TELL IF THERE WERE AT LEAST OBVIOUS HARMFUL PHYSICAL EFFECTS WOULD BE TO COMPARE TWO UNMIXED RACIAL POPULATIONS WITH A POPULATION WHICH IS A MIXTURE OF THE TWO. FOR EXAMPLE NATIVE POPULATIONS OF SWEDEN AND SUDAN COULD SERVE AS THE UNMIXED POPULATIONS AND THAT OF EGYPT AS THE MIXED POPULATION. THE COMPARISON IS NOT PERFECT, BUT IT IS PRETTY CLOSE. IT SEEMS THAT SIMPLE TEST WOULD BE ABLE TO ILLUMINATE THE MATTER, SUCH AS THE FREQUENCY OF POOR VISION AMONG 10 YEAR OLDS IN THE THREE GROUPS AND THE FREQUENCY OF ILL-SPACED TEETH.
THERE IS OF COURSE AN AESTHETIC SIDE. IN "STUDIES IN THE PSYCHOLOGY OF SEX" HAVELOCK ELLIS PROPORTS THAT THE ATTRACTIVENESS OF PEOPLES IS A FUNCTION OF HOW MUCH THEY REFLECT
THE RACIAL TYPE TO WHICH INDIVIDUALS BELONG. THIS SHOULD BE SUBJECT TO EMPERICAL, THOUGH SUBJECTIVE, EVALUATION BY COMPARING, SAY, SWEDISH WITH PUERTO RICAN AND EGYPTIAN AND CENTRAL AFRICAN FACIAL ATTRACTIVENESS AMONG VARIOUS POPULATIONS OF THE WORLD. OF COURSE THERE ARE BEAUTIFUL WOMEN IN ALL THE GROUPS, BUT THE QUESTION IS FREQUENCY. IF MEN IN PUERTO RICO OR EGYPT FOUND SWEDISH OR CENTRAL AFRICAN WOMEN MORE ATTRACTIVE THAN LOCAL WOMEN, IT SEEMS IT WOULD HAVE SIGNIFICANCE FOR THE FUTURE HAPPINESS OF MANKIND AND ARGUE AGAINST INTERRACIAL BREEDING. THAT IS THE ARGUMENT IN "GULLIVER'S TRAVELS" THAT THE SMART HORSES MADE.
ASHLEY MONTEGU ENGAGED IN NO EMPIRICAL TESTS, OBJECTIVE OR SUBJECTIVE, TO SUKPPORT HIS CONTENTION THAT RACIAL MIXING WOULD ENHANCE THE MIXED GROUP'S WELL-BEING. HIS "STUDY" IS IN REALITY NOTHING MORE THAN A STATEMENT OF HIS PARTICULAR OPINION, PERHAPS THE RESULT OF PREJUDICE. THE AURA OF SCIENCE WITH WHICH HE PRESENTS HIS ARGUMENT IS ALL AURA AND NO SCIENCE.
top_screaming.title: AN INTERESTING TOPIC, BUT NOT A SCIENTIFIC BOOK

top_screaming.text:
ABOUT FIVE YEARS AGO I DECIDED TO BROADEN MY HORIZONS AND READ SOME SCIENCE FICTION AND FANTASY. I BEGAN BY RESEARCHING NOVELS THAT WON BOTH THE HUGO AND NEBULA AWARDS IN THE SAME YEAR AND MOVED ON FROM THERE. THE MAJORITY OF THESE AWARD WINNING NOVELS ARE SEGMENTS IN A SERIES, AND MORE OFTEN THAN NOT THE FIRST SEGMENT. THERE ARE EXCEPTIONS, ENDERS GAME, AND SEQUEL SPEAKER FOR THE DEAD BOTH WHERE HONORED THROUGH WINNING BOTH OF THESE PRESTIGIOUS SCIENCE FICTION AND FANTASY AWARDS. THE OTHER BOOKS IN THE SERIES WERE REMARKABLE AS WELL. I HAVE FOUND THAT TO BE TRUE WITH MANY OF THESE SERIES, AND I AM NOW A FULLY DEDICATED SCIENCE FICTION AND FANTASY READER.

I HAVE READ MOST OF THE GREATS. I DEFINE GREATNESS IN THESE CLOSELY RELATED GENRES THROUGH IMMORTALITY....STORIES THAT WILL LIVE FOREVER, AND THE AUTHORS NAMES THAT ARE STAMPED TO THEM. THERE ARE MANY TO CHOSE FROM, FOR ME IT WAS ASIMOV AND THE FOUNDATION NOVELS, DAN SIMMONS AND THE HYPERION QUARTET (PROFOUNDLY UNDER RATED) AND TO A SLIGHTLY LESSER EXTENT THE ORIGINAL FRANK HERBERT DUNE NOVELS, AND CARD'S ENDER NOVELS. I AM LEAVING OUT SO MANY AND I WOULD BE REMISS TO LEAVE OUT TOLKIN AND THE LORD OF THE RING NOVELS WHICH ARE UPPER ECHELON HALL OF FAMERS.

RECENTLY IN READING THROUGH THE GAME OF THRONES NOVELS, I THOUGHT GEORGE RR MARTIN MAY HAVE ESTABLISHED HIMSELF FIRMLY AT THE TOP OF THE LIST. ALL OF THAT IS NOW IN THE PAST BELONGING TO A DIFFERENT ERA, A TIME BEFORE KVOTHE WAS INTRODUCED TO THE WORLD.

PATRICK ROTHFUSS BECAME AN IMMORTAL IN THE FIRST 100 PAGES OF THE NAME OF THE WIND. THIS NOVEL IS THE FIRST OF A TRILOGY WHICH INVOLVES AN ENIGMATIC AND SEEMINGLY DESPONDENT INN-KEEPER WHO IS ACTUALLY A POWERFUL WIZARD OR ARCANIST. HE IS A LEGEND, THE SUBJECT OF COUNTLESS TALES AND ADVENTURES. HE IS ALSO PRESUMED DEAD. EVENTUALLY A CHRONICLER IS INTRODUCED TO THE STORY AND PROCEEDS TO APPEAR IN THE INN (BY FATE OR DESIGN) AND RECOGNIZES THE FACT THAT THIS INN-KEEPER IS ACTUALLY KVOTHE, AND WITH THE HELP OF KVOTHE'S PROTEGEE, A MEMBER OF THE FAY NAMED BAST, THE IN-KEEPER IS FINALLY CONVINCED TO SET THE RECORD STRAIGHT. HE TELLS THE CHRONICLER THAT IT WILL TAKE 3 DAYS TO TELL HIS STORY WITH COMPLETE ACCURACY, AND EACH NOVEL IN THE TRILOGY IS TO REPRESENT ONE DAY. THERE MIGHT BE SOME CYNICS OUT THERE WHO WILL ARGUE THAT IT TAKES MORE THAN ONE NOVEL TO MAKE AN AUTHOR AN ALL TIME GREAT, AND THERE IS SOMETHING TO BE SAID FOR THAT. ROTHFUSS WENT AHEAD AND FOLLOWED HIS MASTERPIECE WITH ANOTHER IN THE SECOND BOOK ENTITLED THE WISEMAN'S FEAR. QUALITY TRILOGIES ARE SAID TO BE RARE. IT TAKES SOMEONE SPECIAL TO MAKE A SEQUEL FLOW WITH THE SAME PACE AND LANGUAGE AS THE ORIGINAL. ROTHFUSS TOOK HIS TIME IN PERFECTING THE SEQUEL. HE WAS EAGER TO HEAR AS MUCH FEEDBACK AS POSSIBLE FROM READERS, LISTENING CAREFUL WHAT THEY WANTED MORE OF, AND EVEN WHAT THEY WANTED LESS OFF IN TERMS OF ASPECTS THAT ENDEARED THEM TO THE ORIGINAL. THE MOST PAINSTAKING ASPECT OF THESE NOVELS IS HAVING TO WAIT FOR THE THIRD EDITION. THE LIST OF QUESTIONS THAT BEGAN TO MOUNT FROM THE EARLIEST PAGES OF THE THE NAME OF THE WIND REMAINED AT THE CLOSE OF DAY 2 IN THE WISEMAN'S FEAR. NAMELY HOW DID KVOTHE, A LEGEND OF EARTH SHATTERING PROPORTIONS END UP AN IN-KEEPER WHO HAS SEEMINGLY GIVEN UP ON LIFE.

THE STORY IS INCREDIBLY COMPLEX, TO THE EXTENT THAT I WILL NOT ATTEMPT TO SUMMARIZE ANYMORE OF THE SCENE THAN I ALREADY HAVE. WHAT I WILL SAY IS THAT THIS BOOK IS WRITTEN ON ANOTHER LEVEL. THE NOVELS I MENTIONED EARLIER SUCH AS THE FOUNDATION, DUNE, ENDER, AND HYPERION WERE MIND BOGGLING STORIES OF THE SORT THAT IT PAINED ME TO HAVE TO PUT THE BOOKS DOWN TO MEET THE BASIC NECESSITIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF DAILY LIFE. GAME OF THRONES AGAIN, AN EPOCH ENTHRALLING STORY AND CHARACTERS THAT WILL LIVE FOREVER.

HOWEVER, IF WE ARE BEING COMPLETELY HONEST WE MUST SAY THAT PATRICK ROTHFUSS WRITES ON ANOTHER STRATOSPHERE. FORGET CHARACTER DEVELOPMENT, DETAIL, AND DESCRIPTION WHICH HE IS HANDS DOWN THE BEST OF ANYONE IN ANY GENRE OF LITERATURE (MY HUMBLE, YET UNWAVERING OPINION). ROTHFUSS IMAGINED AND WROTE FOR US THE FIRST TWO SEGMENTS OF WHAT SHOULD ONE DAY BE CATEGORIZED AS CLASSIC LITERATURE, MANDATORY READING FOR ENGLISH AND LITERATURE MAJORS AT ALL UNIVERSITIES. ROTHFUSS NOT ONLY TANTALIZES US WITH A STORY IN WHICH DEMANDS EVERY OUNCE OF OUR ATTENTION, BUT HE ALSO WRITES IN A FASHION THAT MAKES WORDS READ AND APPEAR BEAUTIFUL. THIS IS A DAY AND AGE IN WHICH A GOOD STORY OFTEN MASKS POOR TECHNICAL WRITING. THERE ARE THOSE WHO ARE GOOD AT THE ART OF STORYTELLING BUT LACKING IN THE SKILL OF LANGUAGE. IF ANYBODY EVER DESERVED TO BE TOLD THAT THERE NOVEL WAS THE HIGHEST QUALITY OF POETRY, MUSIC, AND LYRICAL VERSE IN MOTION, IT IS PATRICK ROTHFUSS. HIS WORK READS AS BEAUTIFULLY AS JOHN FOWLES IN THE MAGUS. THE FIRST TWO 2 DAYS IN ROTHFUSS'S KINGKILLER CHRONICLES ARE NOVELS THAT RAISE THE STANDARD FOR STORY TELLERS AND DEFINE AN ERA OF LITERATURE. THE NAME OF THE WIND, AND THE WISEMAN'S FEAR ARE EVERYTHING I HAVE SAID AND MORE. IT IS NEARLY IMPOSSIBLE TO IMAGINE ANOTHER NOVEL MEASURING UP TO THESE PRIOR TO THE RELEASE OF THE THIRD ONE. IT'S GOING TO BE A WHILE, BUT IT WILL BE WORTH IT REGARDLESS OF TIME. READ THESE TWO NOVELS, SOAK THEM UP, TAKE THEM IN, CONSIDER EVERYTHING THAT MAKES A STORY GREAT, AND ALL THAT IS INVOLVED IN CREATING BEAUTY THROUGH THE WRITTEN WORD, AND YOU WILL REALIZE THAT YOU ARE EXPERIENCING A MASTER AUTHOR'S ETERNAL CONTRIBUTION TO THE WORLD

WORD OF THE DOVE, FOR WHAT IT'S WORTH

With sincere hope and peace,

Agnes "Sparky" Sparnecht

Deerfield Beach, Florida

THE AUTHORS DELVED INTO THE ILL ATTEMPTED COUP OF APRIL 11, 2002

top_screaming.text:
I WAS FEELING SO VERY SAD FOR IRA HAYES, ALSO, THE BLOCKING OF ONE OF THE TRUE FLAG RAISERS WAS
HARLON BLOCK, BECAUSE THIS POLITICIAN THOUGHT OF ONLY OF RAISING MONEY FOR THE WAR BONDS
AND IT BEING TOO LATE TO NAME HARLON BLOCK. WHAT A SHAME HIS MOTHER COULD NOT BE HONORED
AS WAS THE OTHER MOTHERS WHO WERE STILL ALIVE, BUT HARLON BLOCKS MOTHER KNEW IT WAS HIM.
WHEN HER OTHER SON ASKED WHY, I CHANGED HIS DIAPERS AS A BABY, AND I KNOW HIS BEHIND VERY
WELL. SO, NO ONE IS GOING TO ME THIS IS NOT MY SON. AFTER TELLING THIS TO HIS YOUNGER BROTHER
HE RAN CALLING HIS FATHER WHO WAS IN ANOTHER ROOM TO TELL HIM THIS. JAMES BRADLY, WAS ALSO
A TRUE HERO, HIS LATER YEARS WHEN HE WAS ON THE STAIRS, WITH HIS GRANDSON, WHERE ARE THEY, WHERE ARE THEY!!!! IT MADE ME AND MY HUSBAND CRY. MY HUSBAND THEN SAID TO ME, DOESN'T THIS REMIND US OF YOUR UNCLE CARMINE, WE WERE AT A WEDDING ON MY MOTHES SIDE OF THE FAMILY AND
MY HUSBAND WAS IN A CONVERSATION WITH HIM, SAYING, UNCLE CARMINE, DIDN'T YOU JOIN THE SERVICE
AT THAT TIME, THEN, AND YOU HAVE NEVER TALKED ABOUT. HE ANSWERED YES. MY HUSBAND ASKED WHERE
DID YOU FIGHT, UNCLE CARMINE SAID, IN THE BATTLE OF THE BULDGE. WHAT!! THE BATTLE OF THE BULDGE WAS A MAJOR BATTLE, AND YOU HAVE NEVER SAID ANYTHING ABOUT IT, WHAT ABOUT YOUR WIFE, AUNT CARMELA, DOES SHE KNOW, HE SAID NO. YOU MUST HAVE GOTTEN MEDALS FOR THAT BATTLE, HE SAID YES,
I HAVE THEM THROWN IN A DRAWER IN MY HOUSE. MY HUSBAND ASKED UNCLE CARMINE CAN I SEE THEM,
HE SAID YES BECAUSE HE KNEW HE ALSO SERVED IN THE ARMY. WHEN MY HUSBAND, GOT TO HIS HOUSE
SURE ENOUGH THE METALS WERE IN THAT DRAWER, WITH A FLAG, AND THE MEDALS WERE FOR THE COMBAT CROSS AND MANY MANY OTHERS, BEFORE COMING OFF A SMALL BOAT THE HEAD OF THE THEM TOLD THEM ALL THAT BEFORE THEY LAND, TIE THEIR BOOTS AROUND THEIR SHOULDERS, NOT TO GET THEM WET.HE ALSO HAD A FILM THAT HE SHOWED TO MY HUSBAND, A MAN WITH A CAMERA FILMING THEM FROM BEHIND COMPING OFF THE SMALL LANDING CRAFT, YEARS LATER MY UNCLE CARMINE WAS LOOKING FOR THE INFO ON THE MILITARY CHANNEL AND SAW THAT A FILM WAS COMING ON ABOUT THE BATTLE OF THE BULDGE. HE TURNED ON THE CHANNEL, AND SAW, MANY SMALL BOATS COMING OUT OF THE WATER LANDING ON THE
BEACH. ALL OF A SUDDEN HE SEES HIMSELF ALONG WITH ALL THE OTHER MEN WITH THEIR BOOT LACES TIED OVER THEIR BACKS. AS THEY LANDED, THE ONLY DIFFERENCE WAS HE TURNED AROUND AND THE FILM MAKER CAUGHT HIS FACE AS HE LANDED ON THE BEACH AND THE FACE WAS INDEED HIS. MY HUSBAND ASKED HIM NOW IF HE COULD TAKE ALL THE METALS, AND OF THE FILM SHOWING HIM COMING OFF THE RAFT. MY UNCLE FIGHTING IN THE BATTLE OF THE BULDGE, AND ALL THE MEDALS HE RECEIVED. PRINTED HIS NAME, RANK, AND A SMALL INJURY HE HAD. MY HUSBAND LOOKED AT THE FILM, TOOK THE FILM AND THE METALS AND GAVE THEM TO A STORE THAT PUT METALS IN A LONG VELVET BROWN FRAMED BOX AND PUT ALL THE METALS IN IT.
AND THE CERTIFICATE SHOWING HIS TIME IN THE BATTLE OF THE BULDGE. AFTER HE DIED, HIS DAUGHTERS WAS GIVEN THE BOX AS HE HAD NO SONS. THEY ALSO DIED, AND NOW WE DO NOT KNOW WHERE THIS BOX IS.
ALSO, MY HUSBAND THOUGHT IT WAS TIME TO BE CALLED UP, WHEN HE WAS 18 YEARS OLD, HE JOINED THE ARMY. ALSO, HE WAS ONE OF 7 BROTHERS, EACH ENLISTING IN EVERY BRAND OF THE ARMED SERVICES. HIS PARENTS PROUDLY PUT ON THE FRONT DOOR, ALL OF THE SYMBOLS THAT EA ONE SERVED. SADLY, ONE OF
HIS BROTHERS, WAS IN VIETNAM AND GOT AGENT ORANGE, AND SADLY DIED FROM IT. AS THE FAMILY WAS SITTING AROUND THE DINING ROOM TABLE, WHEN HE TOLD US THAT THE DOCTORS ALL TOLD HIM HE DID NOT HAVE LONG TO LIVE.
HE OPENED HIS COLLAR AND UNBUTTONED HIS SHIRT TO SHOW US ALL THE VERY RED SCAR ALONG HIS ENTIRE THROAT. A FEW MONTHS LATER, HE DIED. MY BROTHER ENLISTED ALSO, DURING VIET NAM AND WANTED TO FIGHT, BUT AFTER A TEST THEY GAVE HIM, THEY SAW HE WAS MUCH MORE SUITED TO TAKE THE LAST WISHES OF THE MEN WHO WERE DYING, THEY WANTED HIM TO SEND THEM WHAT HE WROTE AND MAIL IT TO THEM, TILL TODAY HE THINKS ABOUT THEM ALOT. I CALLED THE PROGRESSO COMPANY TO SEND PACKAGES TO THEM, IN MY BROTHERS NAME SO HE COULD GIVE THEM OUT. I CALLED PROGRESSO COMPANY A
NUMBER OF TIMES, AND THEY CONTINUED TO DO SO. GOD BLESS THEM. ALSO MY SON TRIED TO JOIN THE MARINES WHEN HE WAS SEVENTEEN, AND THOUGHT THAT ME AND HIS FATHER WOULD NOT OBJECT TO SIGN PAPERWORK GIVING PERMISSION. WE TOLD HIM NO. PLEASE WAIT WE WANT YOU TO FINISH HIGH SCHOOL FIRST TO GET OUR PERMISSION AND THEN YOU CAN ENLIST IN THE MARINE CORP. WELL EVEN THE MARINE RECRUITER TRIED AND TRIED, AND MY SON KEPT CALLING THE MARINE RECRUITER TO REMIND THEM HIS 18TH BIRTHDAY IS COMING UP. SINCE HE NEVER MENTIONED IT, WE THOUGHT MAYBE HE CHANGED HIS MIND
AND WOULD WANT TO GO ON TO COLLEGE. EXACTLY ON HIS EIGHTEENTH BIRTHDAY, OUR DOORBELL RANG, AN ARMY RECRUITER WAS AT THE DOOR, MY SON WAS ALREADY COMING DOWN THE STAIRS, WITH A KNAP
SACK ON HIS BACK. WHEN WE ASKED HIM WHAT HE WAS AT DOOR FOR, HE LOOKED AT US, AND SAID, MOM, DAD, I'M SORRY BUT I AM OLD ENOUGH NOT TO KNEED YOUR PERMISSION. WHEN WE ASKED HIM WE THOUGHT YOU WANTED TO BE IN THE MARINE CORP, THE ARMY RECRUITER SAID HE WAS THE ONLY ONE
WHO WHO WAITED TO RECRUIT HIM, HE SAID WE WERE THE ONLY PART OF THE MILITARY WHO WAITED FOR HIM. IT MUST HAVE BEEN GOD'S WILL. HE LEARNED TO FLY HELICOPTERS AND WORKS FOR A MAJOR DELIVERY COMPANY. AND SO WE SO ENJOYED READING AND WATCHING THE FILM, "BATTLE OF THE BULDGE"
WE ARE SO GRATEFUL THAT HIS GRANDSON WROTE THIS TRUE STORY.
top_screaming.title: THE TRUE UNTOLD STORY OF THE ACTUAL FLAG RAISERS

top_screaming.text:
FREY'S BOOK IS HANDS DOWN THE WORST BOOK ON ADDICTION I HAVE EVER READ, AND QUITE POSSIBLY EVER WRITTEN FOR THE SIMPLE REASON THAT IT IS IN NO WAY BASED ON FACT.

THE AUTHOR STARTS OUT THE BOOK STATING THAT HE CAME TO ON AN AIRPLANE 20 MINUTES BEFORE IT LANDED. HE HAD A GAPING HOLE IN THE SIDE OF HIS FACE, WAS BLEEDING FROM THE MOUTH AND HEAD, AND WAS COVERNED IN URINE, BLOOD, AND VOMIT, ETC. FIRST OF ALL, NO MAJOR COMMERCIAL AIRLINE IS GOING TO LET SOMEONE IN THAT CONDITION ON AN AIRCRAFT. THAT'S NOT ONLY A RULE AIRLINES FOLLOW, IT IS PART OF F.A.A REGULATIONS. NOT TO MENTION THAT HE WAS UNATTENDED. HE CLAIMS THAT A DOCTOR BROUGHT HIM ON, AND TOLD THE FLIGHT CREW TO JUST LET HIM SLEEP. WHAT KIND OF A DOCTOR PUTS SOMEONE WHO IS CLEARLY IN NEED OF IMMEDIATE MEDICAL CARE ON A PLANE INSTEAD OF TREATING THEM? CERTAINLY NOT A REPECTABLE ONE-BUT HEY, THIS IS FICTION AFTER ALL.

I HAVE BEEN TO HAZELDON, WHERE THIS BOOK TAKES PLACE. FREY WOULD HAVE LASTED ALL OF 1 DAY IN THIS FACILITY IF HE DID ANY OF THE THINGS THAT HE CLAIMS HE DID. I HAVE ALSO BEEN TO TWO OTHER REHABS. IF ONE PATIENT PHYSICALLY ASSAULTS ANOTHER, HARASSES/THREATENS/INTIMIDATES ANOTHER PATIENT OR STAFF MEMBER, THEY ARE DISCHARGED IMMEDIATLEY!!!! THIS IS A 'ZERO TOLERANCE', NO QUESTIONS ASKED POLICY, UNIVERSAL AMONG REHABS. THE FACILITY MUST PROTECT THE WELLFARE & SAFETY OF ALL PATIENTS, AND IF MR. FREY, THE WANNA-BE BADASS IS TO BE BELIEVED HE WAS A THREAT, AND WOULD HAVE BEEN REMOVED. WHAT HE CLAIMS HE DID COULD BE LIKENED TO BEING IN A REGULAR HOSPITAL AND HAVING THE PERSON IN THE NEXT BED,WAKE UP ONE NIGHT, START TO FREAK OUT, THREATEN YOU, PHYSICALLY HURT YOU, THEN PROCEED TO TRASH ALL THE FURNITURE IN THE ROOM, AND THE HOSPITAL STAFF DO NOTHING ABOUT IT. HE WOULD HAVE BEEN SENT TO A PSYCH WARD OR SOMETHING.

HE ALSO NEVER WOULD HAVE BEEN ALLOWED IN THE REHAB TO BEGIN WITH. SOMEONE WHO IS IN THAT KIND OF SHAPE MUST GO THROUGH A MEDICALLY MANAGED DETOX THAT THIS FACILTY DOES NOT OFFER. HE NEEDED MAJOR DENTAL SURGERY, STICHES, AND HIS NOSE RE-SET. ONLY WHEN HE HAD HAD THIS WORK DONE WOULD HE HAVE BEEN ALLOWED IN. PATIENTS MUST HAVE A MEDICAL CLEARANCE IN OPRDER TO BE ADDMITTED.

IF A PATIENT DECIDES THAT THEY ARE NOT GOING TO COMPLY WITH RULES, OR DO THE WORK THAT THEY ARE GIVEN, THEY'RE OUT-IT'S THAT SIMPLE. THERE ARE PLENTY OF PEOPLE OUT THERE WHO DO WANT THE HELP, AND MR. FREY WAS DOING NOTHING MORE THAN TAKING UP SPACE.

A COUNSELOR SPENDS APPROX. 4+ YEARS IN ORDER TO BE CREDENTIALED BY THE STATE IN ORDER TO WORK IN A REHAB. IT IS A LONG, DETAILED PROCESS. NO WAY IN HELL WOULD A COUSELOR DRIVE A PATIENT TO A REHAB TO PICK UP HIS GIRLFRIEND. HE NOT ONLY WOULD HAVE LOST HIS JOB, BUT IT WOULD HAVE ENDED HIS CAREER.

SO FREY ALSO CLAIMS THAT HE KILLED A PRIEST IN PARIS WHO TRIED TO TAKE ADVANTAGE OF HIM IN A CHURCH RECTORY. I WILL NEVER BE ONE TO DEFEND THA CATHOLIC CHURCH FOR ALL ITS COVER-UPS, BUT COME ON, PLEASE!! THIS PART IS JUST SO CONTRIVED AND FAKE, IT'S ALMOST LAUGHABLE. THE SAD PART IS, IT'S NOT LAUGHABLE-THIS IS A SICK BOOK THAT HAS THE POTENTIAL TO DO A LOT OF DAMAGE, ESPECIALLY TO THE PEOPLE WHO DON'T KNOW ANY BETTER.

I BOUGHT THIS BOOK BECAUSE THE HIGHER POWER PART OF THE AA PROGRAM HAS ALWAYS BEEN AN ISSUE FOR ME, ALTHOUGH I DO CONTINUE TO WORK THE PROGRAM ANYWAY. I WAS EXPECTING TO FIND AN ENLIGHTENING TAKE ON HOW TO DEAL WITH THIS, ONLY TO DISCOVER THAT FREY HAD A HIGHER POWER WHETHER HE WAS AWARE OF IT OR NOT: HE USED THE TAO;THAT WAS HIS HIGHER POWER.

IT'S SAD TO THINK THAT THIS BOOK COULD HAVE BEEN AN INSPIRATION. I STOPPED GOING TO MEETINGS FOR 2 YEARS BECAUSE I BECAME TIRED OF THEM. I WANTED SOMETHING DIFFERNET THAT I WAS NOT GETTING FROM AA. I AM BACK THERE NOW, AND IT HAS TAKEN ON A NEW MEANING FOR ME. I CAN SAY THAT FREY MAY HAVE HIS REASONS FOR NOT WANTING AA (EVEN THOUGH HE PRACTICED SOME OF ITS MAJOR PRICIPLES), BUT IT'S FOR ALL THE WRONG REASONS: HE DOESN'T QUITE UNDERSTAND THE PROGRAM, OR WHAT IT'S REALLY ALL ABOUT.

THIS BOOK IS ACTUALLY VERY MEAN SPIRITED AND SADISTIC. FREY KNOWS THIS IS A LIE, AND HE MUST BE THE MOST UNCONSCIONABLE PERSON TO WRITE A BOOK LIKE THIS, SENDING OUT A MESSAGE OF FALSE HOPE AND LIES TO SOMEONE WHO MIGHT BE ON THE VERGE OF ASKING FOR HELP, ONLY TO DECIDE NOT TO AFTER READING THIS PIECE OF TRASH.

OPRAH RECCOMMENDED THIS BOOK OUT OF IGNORANCE. SHE DOESN'T KNOW ANY BETTER. BUT TO THOSE OF US WHO DO, IT'S CLEAR RIGHT FROM AGE 1 THIS BOOK IS BY NO MEANS AMEMIOR-NOT EVEN CLOSE.

I DID NOT WRITE THIS REVIEW TO BE MEAN SPIRITED, AND COMPARE MY STORY WITH FREY'S. I WROTE THIS TO HOPEFULLY EDUCATE SOMEONE, AND MAYBE EVEN HELP SOMEONE BEFORE THEY READ THIS BOOK. IT'S NOT TRUE, AND THERE IS A BETTER WAY!!(NOT NECESSARILY AA EITHER. BUT, PLEASE DON'T TAKE THIS BOOK TO HEART, IT'S NOT WORTHY OF IT.

GOOD LUCK, AND BE WELL EVERYONE.
top_screaming.title: BAD FICTION DISGUISED AS A MEMIOR

top_screaming.text:
THIS IS THE FIRST BOOK BY MELISSA FOSTER THAT I HAVE EVER READ, WHICH INFLUENCED MY FEELINGS A LITTLE WHEN I WAS READING SEIZED BY LOVE BECAUSE IT IS PART OF THIS EXPANSIVE SERIES OF HERS, LOVE IN BLOOM, WHICH HAS THESE MINI-SERIES WITHIN IN IT AND THAT WAS A LITTLE DAUNTING AT FIRST. BUT, ALL I CAN SAY IS THAT I HAVE THE FIRST BOOK IN THIS SERIES, SISTERS IN LOVE, AND I WILL DEFINITELY BE READING THAT ASAP BECAUSE I COULD NOT GET ENOUGH OF THE WORLD THAT WAS DEPICTED IN SEIZED BY LOVE AND IF THE CHARACTERS IN THE OTHER BOOKS IN THIS SERIES ARE JUST AS AMAZING AS THE CHARACTERS IN THIS BOOK THEN I CAN’T WAIT FOR MORE EMOTIONALLY CHARGED AND OVERALL EPIC ROMANCES.

WHILE LIZZIE AND BLUE’S ROMANCE WAS SO ENGROSSING AND ADORABLE AND BASICALLY EVERYTHING I WOULD WANT WHEN I FIND THE PERSON WHO I AM MEANT TO SPEND THE REST OF MY LIFE WITH, THE SETTING OF THE BOOK AND HOW IT TOOK PLACE ON CAPE COD REALLY HIT HOME FOR ME AND HEIGHTENED ALL MY FEELINGS AND EMOTIONS UP A NOTCH. CAPE COD HOLDS A SPECIAL PLACE IN LIZZIE’S HEART BECAUSE SHE GREW UP THERE AND ALWAYS WANTED TO OPEN UP HER FLOWER SHOP IN PROVINCETOWN, OR P-TOWN, AND IT ALSO HOLDS A SPECIAL PLACE IN MY HEART BECAUSE COINCIDENTALLY MY FAMILY IS FROM BREWSTER AND I HAVE BEEN SPENDING MY SUMMERS THERE SINCE I WAS A BABY. THIS CONNECTION JUST MADE MY EXPERIENCE OF READING THIS BOOK SO MUCH MORE BECAUSE I COULD COMPLETELY PICTURE WHAT WAS GOING ON AND THEREFORE INVESTED SO MUCH INTO LIZZIE AND BLUE RELATIONSHIP AS THEY FINALLY REALIZE THEIR FEELINGS FOR EACH OTHER AFTER A YEAR OF KNOWING EACH OTHER AND DECIDE TO GO FOR IT.

LIZZIE BARBER COULD EASILY BE COMPARED TO WONDER WOMAN BECAUSE SHE DOES SO MUCH WITH HER BUSINESS AT HER FLOWER SHOP AND THEN WITH HER SECOND BUSINESS, THE WEBCAST THE NAKED BAKER, AND THEN IN BETWEEN SHE HAS TIME TO BRING THE LEFT OVER FLOWERS FROM HER SHOP TO UNATTENDED GRAVES AT THE CEMETERY AS WELL AS DROPS OFF THE TREATS SHE MAKES TO THE LOCAL HOMELESS SHELTER. SHE IS SELFLESS, KIND, UNBELIEVABLY DETERMINED, AND FIERCELY LOYAL TO HER FAMILY ESPECIALLY HER SISTER MADDY WHOM SHE IS SUPPORTING FINANCIALLY AS SHE ATTENDS COLLEGE. I WAS IN AWE OF HOW MUCH STRENGTH AND GENEROSITY LIZZIE HAD BECAUSE IT MADE HER SO PERSONABLE AND I ADMIRED HER SO MUCH FOR WHAT SHE WAS DOING ESPECIALLY WITH HER WEBCAST SINCE IT WAS NOT SOMETHING SHE WANTED TO CONTINUE DOING BUT SHE DID IT ANYWAY BECAUSE OF HER SISTER. SHE VERY MUCH INSPIRES YOU THROUGH THE WORK SHE DOES BECAUSE REGARDLESS OF HOW SHE FEELS ABOUT CONTINUING TO FILM HER WEBCAST SHE APPROACHES IT FROM A BUSINESS STANDPOINT AND USES HER KNOWLEDGE TO TURN IT INTO A PROFIT THAT WILL HELP HER SISTER. INTELLIGENCE, GENEROSITY, LOYALTY, AND DETERMINATION ARE ALL QUALITIES THAT LIZZIE EMBODIES AND IT WAS SUCH A JOY TO SEE THEM COME TO LIFE THROUGHOUT THE BOOK.

I CAN PRETTY MUCH SUM UP BLUE RYDER WITH ONE WORD: PERFECTION. NOW IT MAY BE BOLD TO USE THAT WORD BUT HONESTLY HE IS PERFECTION. THE MORE I READ, THE MORE I THOUGHT “COULD HE GET ANYMORE PERFECT?” AND THE ANSWER TIME AFTER TIME WAS YES. WHILE I AM NOT COMPLETELY SOLD ON HIS NAME, BLUE AS A PERSON, MORE SPECIFICALLY AS A MAN, COULD PUT A LOT OF THE MALE POPULATION TO SHAME. HIS HONESTY AND THE AFFECTION HE SHOWED TOWARD LIZZIE MADE MY HEART FLUTTER SO MANY TIMES I LOST TRACK. SOME OF HIS CONFESSIONALS TO LIZZIE WERE JUST SO SINCERE AND GENUINE THAT I ALMOST DIDN’T REALLY BELIEVE HIM BECAUSE I DIDN’T HAVE THE PREVIOUS CONTEXT THAT THEY HAD KNOWN EACH OTHER ALREADY FOR A YEAR. ONCE I WAS ABLE TO TAKE THAT INTO CONSIDERATION, THE DEGREE OF SEPARATION I FELT FROM NOT HAVING READ THOSE PREVIOUS MEMORIES BETWEEN THEM QUICKLY FADED AND I FELL FOR BLUE AS HARD AS LIZZIE DID IN THE BOOK. I DARE SOMEONE TO READ THIS BOOK AND TRY NOT TO FALL IN LOVE WITH BLUE BECAUSE IT IS NOT POSSIBLE. MEN SHOULD TAKE NOTES BECAUSE BLUE CAN ABSOLUTELY SHOW THEM HOW IT IS DONE.

OBVIOUSLY NOT READING ANY OF THE PREVIOUS BOOKS IN THE LOVE IN BLOOM SERIES IMPACTED MY READING OF SEIZED BY LOVE A LITTLE BUT I HONESTLY THINK THAT YOU ARE STILL ABLE TO GAIN ENOUGH CONTEXT THAT BY THE MIDDLE OF THE BOOK THAT DOES NOT EVEN MATTER. THE SETTING, THE CHARACTERS, AND THE ROMANCE WHICH WAS BOTH ADORABLY HEARTWARMING AND WONDERFULLY GENUINE WILL PULL YOU IN FROM THE VERY BEGINNING LEAVING YOU ENGROSSED IN LIZZIE AND BLUE’S RELATIONSHIP AS THEY TACKLE OBSTACLES THAT ARISE OVERCOMING THEM AND DISCOVERING HOW POWERFUL IT IS WHEN YOU FIND THE ONE YOU ARE MEANT TO BE WITH. THEY ARE SEIZED BY THEIR LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER AND I GUARANTEE YOU WILL BE TOO AS YOU WITNESS HOW KNOWING EACH OTHER FOR A YEAR SET THEM UP FOR A LOVE THAT WILL LAST THEM THE REST OF THEIR LIVES.
top_screaming.title: A heartwarmingly adorable and wonderfully genuine romance!!

top_screaming.text: I READ THE HUNGER GAMES TWO YEARS AGO AND NEVER HAD A CHANCE TO WRITE A REVIEW. I WASN'T ONE OF THE EARLIEST FANS BUT I READ THE BOOK IN 2011 AND TO BE HONEST I WASN'T A BOOKS PERSON BACK THEN. BUT AFTER I READ THE HUNGER GAMES, I HAD THIS SUDDEN URGE TO READ MORE AND MORE OF ANYTHING. SO I ASKED MY FRIENDS ABOUT RECOMMENDATIONS, I WENT TO THE LIBRARY AND CHECKED THE INTERNET AND PRETTY SOON THERE WERENT MANY BOOKS LEFT THAT I HAVENT READ REALLY. HUNGER GAMES REALY GAVE ME THE LOVE OF READING. I REALLY LEARNED THE MEANING OF READING ONCE I READ THESE BOOKS.
